body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501297014%2501Senate%2bUpdate_%2bFighting%2bTrump%25e2%2580%2599s%2bCorruption%2band%2bSupporting%2bMaryland%2bFamilies%250115361.49145740.7234370.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501Friend%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501297014%2501Senate%2bUpdate_%2bFighting%2bTrump%25e2%2580%2599s%2bCorruption%2band%2bSupporting%2bMaryland%2bFamilies%250115361.49145740.7234370.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501Friend%2501)
---|---
| | | **June 6, 2025** |
---|---|---|---
| | |
# Senate Update: Fighting Trump's Corruption and Supporting Maryland Families
As Americans face high costs on everything from their groceries to childcare, Donald Trump is ignoring these challenges in favor of blatant, self-interested corruption and lawlessness. He's selling direct access to the presidency to anyone who buys his [meme coin](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2f4oJ66MTnFtQ&redir_log=260407344699285), launching a family stablecoin, USD1, that is being used for a $2 billion deal with a state-owned company in the United Arab Emirates, and accepting a $400 million jumbo jet from Qatar. As he curries favor with billionaires and authoritarian regimes, Trump and Republicans in Congress are working to finance their tax giveaway for the ultra-rich by gutting Medicaid, food and nutrition programs, and much more while still driving deficits through the roof. As they try to ram their big, ugly bill through Congress, Trump and his cronies continue to flout our laws, defy court orders, and threaten to suspend habeas corpus.

All this comes from the candidate who promised he'd stand up for forgotten Americans and those struggling to make ends meet. This is the Great Betrayal. Donald Trump is selling America out for his own personal gain and for that of his billionaire buddies. We must keep fighting back.
---
## Fight in Congress
### Battling the Trump/Republican Billionaire Tax Giveaway
On May 22, House Republicans passed the centerpiece of the Trump legislative agenda--a tax giveaway for the ultra-rich that Trump calls his "Big, Beautiful Bill." He's right that this bill is big, but you can't call it beautiful unless you're a billionaire. For anyone else, it's downright ugly.

Their big, ugly bill doubles down on Trump's 2017 tax giveaway for the uber-rich and mega-corporations and mostly puts those tax breaks on the national credit card, blowing up the national debt by trillions of dollars, which could drive up interest rates. The latest score from the non-partisan Congressional Budget Office says deficits would rise by about $3 trillion in the next decade. When the government borrows so much money, interest rates can rise for everyone--leaving Americans to face higher interest costs on mortgages and car loans.

While much of their plan is unpaid for, Republicans are bankrolling the rest of the bill's high costs by slashing Medicaid, food and nutrition (SNAP), and other programs that help tens of millions of American families get by. According to a preliminary analysis, the Trump/Republican plan would cut over $800 billion from Medicaid and the Affordable Care Act as well as another $300 billion from SNAP. The size of these cuts, which will devastate working families living paycheck to paycheck, roughly equals the size of this bill's aggregate tax giveaways for people with incomes above $500,000 a year. According to an analysis by the Penn Wharton Budget Model, the bill would effectively cost 40% of American families an average of $870 in its first year, while those in the top 0.1% would gain, on average, $389,000.

If the GOP gets their way, almost 16 million people would lose their health insurance altogether. Families will face decisions like whether they can afford to pay medical bills or heating bills. Seniors will get kicked out of nursing homes. People with disabilities will lose services that help them live independently and thrive in their communities. Every day, more Marylanders contact my office to share how these Medicaid cuts will devastate their families and lives. I recently joined a vigil to protect Medicaid where I shared [the story of Mary](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dhe4is-pjHbU%26t%3d179s&redir_log=273442144648361), a mother in Carroll County whose adult son is battling colon cancer. Mary wrote to me, "If they take his Medicaid away, it will be a death sentence."

Republicans are also taking an axe to SNAP, which helps low-income families pay for groceries, and they are slashing the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP), which helps Americans afford their utility bills. 40 million Americans will get less food assistance every month, and energy bills will increase by double digits for low-income families.

These cuts to Medicaid, SNAP and LIHEAP will hit millions of families all at once, forcing them to face higher costs at the grocery store and increased energy bills, all while they are stripped of affordable health insurance. What's more, some Americans risk losing their jobs to this bill: it threatens 800,000 good-paying jobs in the clean energy economy, including more than 2,500 in Maryland.

The Trump/Republican bill also targets patriotic federal employees, who are already reeling from the onslaught of chaos and harm caused by Elon Musk and DOGE. Specifically, the bill cuts their pay through a sleight of hand. New federal employees would have to pay extra out of their salary to receive retirement benefits and civil service protections that all civil servants previously received--and should receive--by default.

Over the past few months, we've been pushing back against this big betrayal with every tool we've got. Still, House Republicans fell in line with Trump and passed the bill by a one-vote margin. While Senate Republicans have indicated that they will not adopt the House version as-is, they've said their changes will be small. The House bill also contains several non-budget provisions that are both harmful and will, I believe, fail to meet the requirements of the reconciliation process, the procedural mechanism that allows Senate Republicans to pass this bill despite unanimous Democratic opposition. This includes a provision to block federal courts from enforcing contempt citations--effectively limiting our courts' ability to hold Trump and his Administration in contempt--and another that places a 10-year moratorium on states putting guardrails on artificial intelligence.

Defeating what even Elon Musk (looks like he got at least a temporary dose of truth serum!) rightly calls an "abomination" will not be easy. We must continue to wage an all-out campaign against it. Americans across the country are already mobilizing to stop this bill, and I am confident that the more they learn about it, the more they will oppose it.

I take heart from the fact that, eight years ago, we blocked the Trump/Republican effort to destroy the Affordable Care Act (ACA). Then, as now, they tried to use the reconciliation process, but we defeated that effort by just one vote in the United States Senate. That victory came after a massive groundswell of nationwide activism--countless postcards, protests, and more. Every bit of effort counted then, and every bit of effort will count now.
### Stopping the Cash for Collusion Scheme in El Salvador
Right now, Donald Trump is using your taxpayer dollars to pay the Bukele-run government of El Salvador to lock up U.S. residents in infamous prisons without any due process. This is a conspiracy to deprive individuals living in America of their constitutional rights. That was made abundantly clear from the conversation I had with Salvadoran Vice President, Felix Ulloa--video of which I [recently released here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3d7YlCTjGCH20&redir_log=639245358853284). That's why Senator Tim Kaine and I used a special Senate procedure to force a vote on a resolution we introduced to require the Trump Administration to release a report on their compliance with federal court orders related to the deportation of people to El Salvador and to provide information on the human rights situation there. I argued on [the Senate floor](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dRU_I9Lne6Hk%26t%3d2s&redir_log=441530244273348) that the Senate should, at a minimum, require the executive branch to provide the American public with facts and information regarding the use of their taxpayer dollars.

Disturbingly, however, Republicans blocked our resolution in a party-line vote. They chose to bury their heads in the sand rather than expose the ugly reality of the Trump-Bukele scheme. But I'm not done fighting for the truth and the Constitution.
### Trump Is Enriching Himself As He Undermines American Interests Abroad
A few weeks ago, Donald Trump traveled to Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates (UAE), and Qatar where, instead of focusing on American foreign policy priorities, he spent his time selling out important American interests as he and his family cut deals to personally enrich themselves. A week before President Trump arrived in the UAE, his son Eric Trump went there to get $2 billion from a UAE government-backed fund for the Trump family's new stablecoin venture, World Liberty Financial's USD1. Then, when President Trump arrived, he signed an agreement giving the UAE access to the crown jewels of American AI and semiconductor technology. That same week, Trump also authorized the sale of advanced US weaponry to the UAE, even as the UAE continues to supply weapons to a murderous militia group, the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), which the US government has determined is committing genocide in Sudan.

Meanwhile, as Eric Trump worked to make Trump hotel deals in Qatar, President Trump solicited a jumbo jet from Qatar's government to use as his new Air Force One (and potentially later for his presidential library) and approved a large arms sale. While Trump was cutting these financial deals in the Gulf states, he said and did virtually nothing about the humanitarian disaster in Gaza and the need to release all of the hostages. Candidate Trump said he would swiftly end the war in Gaza and bring back all the hostages. He has done neither.

The spectacle of an American president and his family enriching themselves while giving away cutting-edge American technology, selling advanced weapons to a country abetting the genocide in Sudan, and breaking promises on the war in Gaza is a national embarrassment. The presidency should not be sold to the highest bidder, and American foreign policy decisions shouldn't be made on a pay-to-play basis. Congress must step in. That's why Senator Chris Murphy and I filed joint resolutions of disapproval against impending arms sales to both the [UAE](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-murphy-sanders-kaine-schatz-file-joint-resolutions-of-disapproval-on-16b-in-arms-sales-to-united-arab-emirates&redir_log=447816911011214) and [Qatar](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-joins-murphy-sanders-kaine-schatz-in-filing-joint-resolution-of-disapproval-on-19b-arms-sale-as-qatar-seeks-to-gift-luxury-jumbo-jet-to-trump&redir_log=074246850921431). These bills, if enacted, would block these arms sales from moving forward. These are just a few of the efforts we're pursuing in the Congress to stop Trump's naked corruption. The world's greatest military and economic superpower should not be turned into a grift machine for Donald Trump.

Trump's corruption abroad and his shredding of the Constitution at home has been enabled by Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Recently, in a Senate hearing, I [confronted Rubio directly](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dZSAsZhRJRMY&redir_log=863063345077293) about his complicity in dismantling the Constitution and abandoning the fight for human rights as part of our foreign policy.
### Advancing and Protecting Vital Maryland Priorities
[Maryland is ground zero for many of Trump's illegal efforts to hollow out the federal government and rip away services from working families](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dx_W6mN5ra9E&redir_log=278120971339292). That also means that federal Team Maryland is the tip of the spear in our efforts to fight back. Every day, I'm working with our federal delegation to fight back against Trump's reckless actions that are hurting our communities.

Earlier this year, the Trump Administration halted classes at the National Fire Academy in Emmitsburg, Maryland, which provides lifesaving training to firefighters from around the country. The decision to abruptly cancel these classes made no sense, and it endangered our communities and those who protect them. So, I worked with Representative McClain Delaney and Senator Alsobrooks to get classes restored. [On May 8, I pressed Homeland Security Secretary Noem for answers](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvideo%2ftranscript-van-hollen-presses-dhs-secretary-noem-on-maryland-based-national-fire-academy_gets-commitment-for-funding-to-resume&redir_log=27930291021304) and secured a commitment for funding to resume--and now it has. While the good work done by the men and women at the National Fire Academy never should have been halted, I'm glad to see the Administration has heeded our calls to lift this senseless ban. Classes have now restarted in Emmitsburg.

Unfortunately, attacking the Fire Academy is just one of the ways that Trump is endangering Marylanders' safety. In April, the Administration completely canceled FEMA's Building Resilient Infrastructure and Communities (BRIC) Grant program, taking away $1 billion that had been promised to help Chesapeake Bay watershed communities like Crisfield and South Baltimore and halting low-interest loans to prevent flooding in places like Ellicott City. Every day we don't effectively prepare for new floods and storms is another day that people's lives and livelihoods are put at risk. That's why I [led a bipartisan effort with 85 Democratic and Republican colleagues](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-tillis-murray-murkowski-lead-bipartisan-bicameral-letter-calling-on-dhs-to-reinstate-disaster-mitigation-program-critical-to-local-communities&redir_log=246716734481456) to demand that Trump swiftly restore BRIC and let FEMA do its job: saving lives. [We will keep up this fight.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fbsky.app%2fprofile%2fdid%3aplc%3aht3ruh74w6krts2fz4ir27pa%2fpost%2f3lqdnyzuj3k2o&redir_log=060703933629523)

In addition to cancelling FEMA's BRIC program, the Trump Administration has also denied FEMA support to communities across the country that have been hard-hit by natural disasters, and I am keeping a close watch on their response to Western Maryland's severe flooding. The State of Maryland submitted a request to FEMA to complete Joint Preliminary Damage Assessments in the impacted areas of Western Maryland. That damage assessment is currently underway. In the meantime, as discussed more below, I was [pleased to get a commitment from the head of the Small Business Administration](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dTdawYcZj6bU&redir_log=20546607766298) (SBA) that they would quickly use their own authorities to provide low-cost SBA loans to homeowners and businesses that suffered large losses in the flooding, and [they have since followed through](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-alsobrooks-mcclain-delaney-encourage-residents-to-seek-assistance-through-the-us-small-business-administration-in-wake-of-severe-flash-flooding&redir_log=083502409911458).

I'm also continuing to advance Maryland priorities like protecting the Chesapeake Bay. In a hearing, I [pressed Trump's EPA Administrator, Lee Zeldin, on the need to fund the critical Chesapeake Bay Program](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-secures-commitment-from-epa-administrator-zeldin-to-continue-funding-chesapeake-bay-program&redir_log=700460892441095)--which we successfully defended against proposed elimination during the first Trump Administration. I was glad to receive his commitment to maintain this funding, and I'm pleased to report that the budget they submitted last week does so. While I secured that commitment, I am continuing to battle EPA Administrator Zeldin's ongoing, outrageous, and illegal efforts to dismantle the Greenhouse Gas Reduction Fund (GGRF), a green energy initiative I spent years advocating for and finally passed into law as a part of the Inflation Reduction Act. The GGRF is a win-win-win--reducing energy costs for consumers, generating good-paying jobs, and reducing polluting emissions--but the Trump Administration has launched an illegal effort to dismantle it. As I pointed out in the hearing with Zeldin, the federal courts are exposing the gap between what the EPA claims in public and what they admit to in court. That's an ongoing theme of the Trump Administration as they mislead the American people.

The fight to counter Trump's attacks on Maryland's federal workers continues. This Administration will stop at nothing to undermine our patriotic civil servants--the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) even fired its constituent service liaisons who pick up the phone when Members of Congress want to help a federal worker with human resources-related matters, such as obtaining earned retirement benefits, disability retirement benefits, and survivor's benefits. In response, [I led 15 Senate colleagues](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-colleagues-press-opm-on-firings-of-constituent-services-liaisons&redir_log=862750914487908) in a letter to OPM demanding answers on the firings, and I'll work to ensure OPM continues functioning effectively for our federal workforce and retirees.

When Trump goes after federal workers in Maryland, the whole country suffers. That's why I also recently joined consumer advocates to expose Trump's [attack on the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC)](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2fsGg-opSiyc8&redir_log=508943643203293)--the Maryland-based independent agency that ensures that cribs are safe, toys don't have lead, and so much more. The CPSC has helped save thousands of lives by helping protect families from dangerous and defective products. Now, Trump is trying to shut it down--doing the bidding of corporate interests who put profits over people. Make no mistake: this will endanger lives. That's dangerous idiocy, not efficiency.
## Fight in the Courts
As Trump faces over 250 lawsuits against his illegal actions, he's doing everything he can to sabotage the entire legal system itself in order to prevent the courts from holding him accountable. Here are just a few updates about the ongoing battle in America's courtrooms.
* On May 30, an appellate court preserved a May 22 order from a lower court granting a preliminary injunction halting the Trump Administration's unconstitutional and illegal attempts to reorganize the federal government through huge reductions in force--a.k.a. Trump and DOGE's across-the-board mass firings. The judge stated in her order, "Agencies may not conduct large-scale reorganizations and reductions in force in blatant disregard of Congress's mandates, and a president may not initiate large-scaled executive branch reorganization without partnering with Congress." The Administration has appealed this injunction, but this is an important ruling for our federal employees. In a similar fashion, on June 4, another appellate court preserved a lower court injunction that's blocking Trump from dismantling the Department of Education.
* Unfortunately, on May 19, an appeals court lifted a temporary restraining order that had been stopping Trump from stripping certain federal employees' collective bargaining rights. This clears the way for Trump to commence part of his union-busting plan while an underlying lawsuit continues.
* There are also a slew of legal efforts against Elon Musk and DOGE for sabotaging the government. Democratic state attorneys general have filed a lawsuit arguing that Musk's appointment and the entire DOGE operation are grossly illegal violations of the Constitution's appointments clause, and on May 27, a judge denied a motion to broadly dismiss the case, though President Trump was removed as a defendant. The lawsuit still needs to be fully heard on the merits, but it's a critical case for preserving our checks and balances.
* On May 29, a federal trade court ruled that many of Trump's tariffs are illegal, an encouraging ruling for millions of families and small businesses already feeling the pain of price increases. The tariffs aren't just a disaster, they're unconstitutional. As I have argued on the [Senate Floor](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3ds58F8_lGYOw%26t%3d7s&redir_log=06518015834349), the President does not have the power to wrongfully twist the law to impose a giant sales tax on the American people. An appellate court has allowed the tariffs to stay in place temporarily as it considers the merits of the case. Stay tuned.
* To avoid the requirements of due process and fair trials, the Trump Administration has also floated suspending habeas corpus, the legal procedure that gives people the right to challenge their unlawful detention. On May 22, a Maryland judge took action to protect that right by barring the Trump Administration from swiftly deporting those who challenge their detention under habeas corpus. This order will give the courts the proper opportunity to evaluate each case and aims to stop the Administration from shipping people off to foreign countries without due process or the ability of courts to intervene. It's a shameful sign of the times when our courts need to repeat the Constitution to the executive branch, but it is very important that the U.S. District Court of Maryland did so.
* That's just one example of how the court battle over Trump's attempts to undermine due process and conduct mass deportations has been very active in recent weeks. On May 16, in a rebuke to Trump, the Supreme Court extended its deportation freeze that is preventing Trump from deporting Venezuelans using obscure wartime emergency powers from the Alien Enemies Act. And on May 21, a federal judge said that Trump violated a court order by deporting people to South Sudan. On June 4, a judge ruled that a March deportation of more than 130 Venezuelans under the Alien Enemies Act likely violated due process rights and ordered the Trump Administration to let these people challenge their removal. These important court decisions are shining a light on Trump's attempts to ignore U.S. law.
* Unfortunately, we've also seen setbacks on the immigration front. On May 19, the Supreme Court allowed Trump to revoke Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for 350,000 Venezuelans fleeing chaos in their home country. On May 30, the Supreme Court also allowed Trump to revoke the humanitarian parole status of more than 500,000 immigrants from Cuba, Haiti, Nicaragua, and Venezuela who were granted permission to stay for urgent humanitarian reasons. Despite these painful rulings, we had a small win on May 30, when a judge blocked the revocation of TPS for several thousand Venezuelans who were covered by the country designation that had been extended into 2026 by the Biden Administration.
* We also had an important victory against Trump's deportation operation on May 28, when a judge ruled that Marco Rubio and the Trump Administration's attempt to deport Mahmoud Khalil, a Columbia graduate student, is likely "unconstitutionally vague." I could not agree more: neither Marco Rubio, nor Donald Trump, nor anyone one else in this country has the power to deport people over their political speech.

## Fight in Our Communities
Beyond everything happening in Washington, I'm talking with Marylanders and working across the state to support our communities.

In good news, the U.S. Naval Academy in Annapolis has [restored nearly all of the 381 books](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fbsky.app%2fprofile%2fvanhollen.senate.gov%2fpost%2f3lprg3kv74k2d&redir_log=613776452072440) that it had [removed from its shelves](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.nytimes.com%2f2025%2f04%2f11%2fus%2fpolitics%2fnaval-academy-banned-books.html&redir_log=985486693751533). This reversal came after I attended a meeting of the Board of Visitors, on which I serve, brought some of the books with me--including Maya Angelou's "I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings"--and moved to end the ban. That's progress. We can't stop fighting back.

Beyond the Naval Academy, I'm committed to supporting all of Maryland's servicemembers and military installations, which are important to our country's national security. On May 19, I joined Senator Alsobrooks, Representative Hoyer, and Representative Elfreth to visit Naval Air Station Patuxent River, a critical asset for our national security. I will continue to support naval aviation research and development, along with the educational opportunities and workforce investments that Pax has catalyzed in Southern Maryland. I was also glad to visit with the Southern Maryland Navy Alliance and tour the University System of Maryland at Southern Maryland's SMART building, including the Matrix Lab. The researchers and students there are doing incredible work, and the collaboration between the Alliance, public-sector, and private-sector partners will help strengthen and build the region's workforce for years to come.

To achieve any goal, from a booming economy to a strong, dynamic workforce, we need to educate the next generation. That's why I recently spoke with parents and educators at the Maryland Head Start Association, which plays a critical role in helping our youngest children succeed. Head Start was created 60 years ago to help low-income families access early education, and since then, the program has changed the lives of over 40 million kids. Trump is trying to sully that legacy and destroy Head Start: he illegally froze and withheld billions of dollars of Head Start funding, and he has closed regional offices around the country. Thanks to ferocious advocacy from Head Start providers and families, the President has not proposed cutting Head Start in his initial proposed budget. But make no mistake, the threat has not passed. I will fight tooth and nail to protect Head Start for our kids.

I also want to address the recent antisemitic hate crimes in Boulder, Colorado and Washington, DC. As we pray for the victims and their families, we must redouble our efforts to fight antisemitism. The perpetrators must be brought to justice, and we must ensure that antisemitism and hate in all their forms have no refuge in America. I've long fought for increased funding to support security measures at synagogues, places of worship, community centers, and other targets for hate crimes. I know many in our communities feel unsafe, even more so following these heinous attacks. While nonprofit security measures are just one tool we have to keep our communities safe, it's one we should be fully utilizing, which is why I [support significantly increasing the funding for this program.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fimo%2fmedia%2fdoc%2ffema_nonprofit_security_grant_program_nsgp_letter.pdf&redir_log=974687488929720)
### Addressing Flooding in Western Maryland
Forty-eight hours after devastating floods hit communities in Western Maryland, I met with local leaders and emergency responders to express my support for their efforts and to assess the damage. Having seen the impact firsthand, it is clear there will be much work ahead to rebuild, and I'm committed to supporting those efforts in any way I can. That's why, as discussed above, I [pressed the head of the Small Business Administration Kelly Loeffler to provide immediate relief to Western Maryland](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dTdawYcZj6bU&redir_log=20546607766298), and I am glad the SBA has [moved quickly to do so.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-alsobrooks-mcclain-delaney-encourage-residents-to-seek-assistance-through-the-us-small-business-administration-in-wake-of-severe-flash-flooding&redir_log=083502409911458)

I encourage all Allegany and Garrett County small businesses, non-profits, homeowners, and renters who need support to apply for resources available to them through the U.S. Small Business Administration's (SBA) Disaster Assistance program. [You can visit the SBA's website here for information on how to apply for disaster loans.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2flending.sba.gov%2fsearch-disaster%2f%3fdisaster%3dPA-20019&redir_log=995442680186383) The deadline to file for physical damage assistance is July 21, 2025.

My heart is with everyone who has suffered damage from the devastating storms, and my sincere gratitude goes out to all the first responders whose heroic work ensured all residents' safety. The unity and resolve of these communities shone through as neighbors and volunteers were out in full force to lend a helping hand. They remind us why we should be proud to be Marylanders and proud to be Americans. Let's keep striving to live up to their example.

Onwards, \- Chris Van Hollen
[Please click here to unsubscribe.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//EsurveyForm.aspx?__cid=SenVanHollen&__sid=100064&__crop=15361.49145740.7234370.7517223)

clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | | June 6, 2025 | ---|---|---|--- | | | Senate Update: Fighting Trump's Corruption and Supporting Maryland Families As Americans face high costs on everything from their groceries to childcare, Donald Trump is ignoring these challenges in favor of blatant, self-interested corruption and lawlessness. He's selling direct access to the presidency to anyone who buys his , launching a family stablecoin, USD1, that is being used for a $2 billion deal with a state-owned company in the United Arab Emirates, and accepting a $400 million jumbo jet from Qatar. As he curries favor with billionaires and authoritarian regimes, Trump and Republicans in Congress are working to finance their tax giveaway for the ultra-rich by gutting Medicaid, food and nutrition programs, and much more while still driving deficits through the roof. As they try to ram their big, ugly bill through Congress, Trump and his cronies continue to flout our laws, defy court orders, and threaten to suspend habeas corpus. All this comes from the candidate who promised he'd stand up for forgotten Americans and those struggling to make ends meet. This is the Great Betrayal. Donald Trump is selling America out for his own personal gain and for that of his billionaire buddies. We must keep fighting back. Fight in Congress Battling the Trump/Republican Billionaire Tax Giveaway On May 22, House Republicans passed the centerpiece of the Trump legislative agenda--a tax giveaway for the ultra-rich that Trump calls his "Big, Beautiful Bill." He's right that this bill is big, but you can't call it beautiful unless you're a billionaire. For anyone else, it's downright ugly. Their big, ugly bill doubles down on Trump's 2017 tax giveaway for the uber-rich and mega-corporations and mostly puts those tax breaks on the national credit card, blowing up the national debt by trillions of dollars, which could drive up interest rates. The latest score from the non-partisan Congressional Budget Office says deficits would rise by about $3 trillion in the next decade. When the government borrows so much money, interest rates can rise for everyone--leaving Americans to face higher interest costs on mortgages and car loans. While much of their plan is unpaid for, Republicans are bankrolling the rest of the bill's high costs by slashing Medicaid, food and nutrition (SNAP), and other programs that help tens of millions of American families get by. According to a preliminary analysis, the Trump/Republican plan would cut over $800 billion from Medicaid and the Affordable Care Act as well as another $300 billion from SNAP. The size of these cuts, which will devastate working families living paycheck to paycheck, roughly equals the size of this bill's aggregate tax giveaways for people with incomes above $500,000 a year. According to an analysis by the Penn Wharton Budget Model, the bill would effectively cost 40% of American families an average of $870 in its first year, while those in the top 0.1% would gain, on average, $389,000. If the GOP gets their way, almost 16 million people would lose their health insurance altogether. Families will face decisions like whether they can afford to pay medical bills or heating bills. Seniors will get kicked out of nursing homes. People with disabilities will lose services that help them live independently and thrive in their communities. Every day, more Marylanders contact my office to share how these Medicaid cuts will devastate their families and lives. I recently joined a vigil to protect Medicaid where I shared , a mother in Carroll County whose adult son is battling colon cancer. Mary wrote to me, "If they take his Medicaid away, it will be a death sentence." Republicans are also taking an axe to SNAP, which helps low-income families pay for groceries, and they are slashing the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP), which helps Americans afford their utility bills. 40 million Americans will get less food assistance every month, and energy bills will increase by double digits for low-income families. These cuts to Medicaid, SNAP and LIHEAP will hit millions of families all at once, forcing them to face higher costs at the grocery store and increased energy bills, all while they are stripped of affordable health insurance. What's more, some Americans risk losing their jobs to this bill: it threatens 800,000 good-paying jobs in the clean energy economy, including more than 2,500 in Maryland. The Trump/Republican bill also targets patriotic federal employees, who are already reeling from the onslaught of chaos and harm caused by Elon Musk and DOGE. Specifically, the bill cuts their pay through a sleight of hand. New federal employees would have to pay extra out of their salary to receive retirement benefits and civil service protections that all civil servants previously received--and should receive--by default. Over the past few months, we've been pushing back against this big betrayal with every tool we've got. Still, House Republicans fell in line with Trump and passed the bill by a one-vote margin. While Senate Republicans have indicated that they will not adopt the House version as-is, they've said their changes will be small. The House bill also contains several non-budget provisions that are both harmful and will, I believe, fail to meet the requirements of the reconciliation process, the procedural mechanism that allows Senate Republicans to pass this bill despite unanimous Democratic opposition. This includes a provision to block federal courts from enforcing contempt citations--effectively limiting our courts' ability to hold Trump and his Administration in contempt--and another that places a 10-year moratorium on states putting guardrails on artificial intelligence. Defeating what even Elon Musk (looks like he got at least a temporary dose of truth serum!) rightly calls an "abomination" will not be easy. We must continue to wage an all-out campaign against it. Americans across the country are already mobilizing to stop this bill, and I am confident that the more they learn about it, the more they will oppose it. I take heart from the fact that, eight years ago, we blocked the Trump/Republican effort to destroy the Affordable Care Act (ACA). Then, as now, they tried to use the reconciliation process, but we defeated that effort by just one vote in the United States Senate. That victory came after a massive groundswell of nationwide activism--countless postcards, protests, and more. Every bit of effort counted then, and every bit of effort will count now. Stopping the Cash for Collusion Scheme in El Salvador Right now, Donald Trump is using your taxpayer dollars to pay the Bukele-run government of El Salvador to lock up U.S. residents in infamous prisons without any due process. This is a conspiracy to deprive individuals living in America of their constitutional rights. That was made abundantly clear from the conversation I had with Salvadoran Vice President, Felix Ulloa--video of which I . That's why Senator Tim Kaine and I used a special Senate procedure to force a vote on a resolution we introduced to require the Trump Administration to release a report on their compliance with federal court orders related to the deportation of people to El Salvador and to provide information on the human rights situation there. I argued on that the Senate should, at a minimum, require the executive branch to provide the American public with facts and information regarding the use of their taxpayer dollars. Disturbingly, however, Republicans blocked our resolution in a party-line vote. They chose to bury their heads in the sand rather than expose the ugly reality of the Trump-Bukele scheme. But I'm not done fighting for the truth and the Constitution. Trump Is Enriching Himself As He Undermines American Interests Abroad A few weeks ago, Donald Trump traveled to Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates (UAE), and Qatar where, instead of focusing on American foreign policy priorities, he spent his time selling out important American interests as he and his family cut deals to personally enrich themselves. A week before President Trump arrived in the UAE, his son Eric Trump went there to get $2 billion from a UAE government-backed fund for the Trump family's new stablecoin venture, World Liberty Financial's USD1. Then, when President Trump arrived, he signed an agreement giving the UAE access to the crown jewels of American AI and semiconductor technology. That same week, Trump also authorized the sale of advanced US weaponry to the UAE, even as the UAE continues to supply weapons to a murderous militia group, the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), which the US government has determined is committing genocide in Sudan. Meanwhile, as Eric Trump worked to make Trump hotel deals in Qatar, President Trump solicited a jumbo jet from Qatar's government to use as his new Air Force One (and potentially later for his presidential library) and approved a large arms sale. While Trump was cutting these financial deals in the Gulf states, he said and did virtually nothing about the humanitarian disaster in Gaza and the need to release all of the hostages. Candidate Trump said he would swiftly end the war in Gaza and bring back all the hostages. He has done neither. The spectacle of an American president and his family enriching themselves while giving away cutting-edge American technology, selling advanced weapons to a country abetting the genocide in Sudan, and breaking promises on the war in Gaza is a national embarrassment. The presidency should not be sold to the highest bidder, and American foreign policy decisions shouldn't be made on a pay-to-play basis. Congress must step in. That's why Senator Chris Murphy and I filed joint resolutions of disapproval against impending arms sales to both the and . These bills, if enacted, would block these arms sales from moving forward. These are just a few of the efforts we're pursuing in the Congress to stop Trump's naked corruption. The world's greatest military and economic superpower should not be turned into a grift machine for Donald Trump. Trump's corruption abroad and his shredding of the Constitution at home has been enabled by Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Recently, in a Senate hearing, I about his complicity in dismantling the Constitution and abandoning the fight for human rights as part of our foreign policy. Advancing and Protecting Vital Maryland Priorities . That also means that federal Team Maryland is the tip of the spear in our efforts to fight back. Every day, I'm working with our federal delegation to fight back against Trump's reckless actions that are hurting our communities. Earlier this year, the Trump Administration halted classes at the National Fire Academy in Emmitsburg, Maryland, which provides lifesaving training to firefighters from around the country. The decision to abruptly cancel these classes made no sense, and it endangered our communities and those who protect them. So, I worked with Representative McClain Delaney and Senator Alsobrooks to get classes restored. and secured a commitment for funding to resume--and now it has. While the good work done by the men and women at the National Fire Academy never should have been halted, I'm glad to see the Administration has heeded our calls to lift this senseless ban. Classes have now restarted in Emmitsburg. Unfortunately, attacking the Fire Academy is just one of the ways that Trump is endangering Marylanders' safety. In April, the Administration completely canceled FEMA's Building Resilient Infrastructure and Communities (BRIC) Grant program, taking away $1 billion that had been promised to help Chesapeake Bay watershed communities like Crisfield and South Baltimore and halting low-interest loans to prevent flooding in places like Ellicott City. Every day we don't effectively prepare for new floods and storms is another day that people's lives and livelihoods are put at risk. That's why I to demand that Trump swiftly restore BRIC and let FEMA do its job: saving lives. In addition to cancelling FEMA's BRIC program, the Trump Administration has also denied FEMA support to communities across the country that have been hard-hit by natural disasters, and I am keeping a close watch on their response to Western Maryland's severe flooding. The State of Maryland submitted a request to FEMA to complete Joint Preliminary Damage Assessments in the impacted areas of Western Maryland. That damage assessment is currently underway. In the meantime, as discussed more below, I was (SBA) that they would quickly use their own authorities to provide low-cost SBA loans to homeowners and businesses that suffered large losses in the flooding, and . I'm also continuing to advance Maryland priorities like protecting the Chesapeake Bay. In a hearing, I we successfully defended against proposed elimination during the first Trump Administration. I was glad to receive his commitment to maintain this funding, and I'm pleased to report that the budget they submitted last week does so. While I secured that commitment, I am continuing to battle EPA Administrator Zeldin's ongoing, outrageous, and illegal efforts to dismantle the Greenhouse Gas Reduction Fund (GGRF), a green energy initiative I spent years advocating for and finally passed into law as a part of the Inflation Reduction Act. The GGRF is a win-win-win--reducing energy costs for consumers, generating good-paying jobs, and reducing polluting emissions--but the Trump Administration has launched an illegal effort to dismantle it. As I pointed out in the hearing with Zeldin, the federal courts are exposing the gap between what the EPA claims in public and what they admit to in court. That's an ongoing theme of the Trump Administration as they mislead the American people. The fight to counter Trump's attacks on Maryland's federal workers continues. This Administration will stop at nothing to undermine our patriotic civil servants--the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) even fired its constituent service liaisons who pick up the phone when Members of Congress want to help a federal worker with human resources-related matters, such as obtaining earned retirement benefits, disability retirement benefits, and survivor's benefits. In response, in a letter to OPM demanding answers on the firings, and I'll work to ensure OPM continues functioning effectively for our federal workforce and retirees. When Trump goes after federal workers in Maryland, the whole country suffers. That's why I also recently joined consumer advocates to expose Trump's Maryland-based independent agency that ensures that cribs are safe, toys don't have lead, and so much more. The CPSC has helped save thousands of lives by helping protect families from dangerous and defective products. Now, Trump is trying to shut it down--doing the bidding of corporate interests who put profits over people. Make no mistake: this will endanger lives. That's dangerous idiocy, not efficiency. name: Lisa Blunt Rochester
subject: Gutting Medicaid is cruelty, plain and simple

Let me be clear, team, Republicans gutting Medicaid is cruel. Plain and simple. Your grandmother, grandfather, your parents, disabled folks, and millions of other Americans are at risk of losing their only line of healthcare. Working people will face new copays and out-of-pocket costs increases while carrying the burden of making ends meet, **_just so Trump's billionaire friends can get a tax break._** If you don't believe me, just look at the math: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109110569/550033479/-499364019?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.06.06_Medicaid_B1_F1_S1_C1_210DONx90DO_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODA1YmJiZS1mYTQyLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=e7iRE2GFNytjCcG6-HIlnikdBq-VowQ5Nfou43hNYfk=) **This is cruel, team.** I won't let extremist Republicans sabotage healthcare and life-saving services that millions of Americans depend on. That's why I'm fighting for you every day in the Senate. Democrats and I are leading the fight against MAGA Republicans' "Big Beautiful Bill" that would hurt the elderly, disabled, and others who need Medicaid while giving their billionaire buddies a tax cut. [**But I can't do this alone and need to know that you're with me. Will you stand with Democrats who are fighting for Medicaid by supporting our effort with a special gift of $5 or more? body:
Their blowup is a huge opportunity for Democrats in PA - with Elon Musk likely on the sidelines, MAGA will no longer have Elon’s billions at their disposal. | | [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-0kKzB4QgF380jLRJclLJi_c31Qhsf2-Z3aTeic_jc86uKxfBBT6rbZglzlEWFS-YyYaElv6YEOIOo2WwNS52rC9Fmw-FdHdhU1DsZ6Wnh7X4-QG2qD52BreGjFGPqiUhLd0bX-GCjcd_PvpuKS_CUECswbyeo0ZTTXXMAuvLNeG3t7kJr9CahIFUoAnbDSywkeYaCpbrWc0EPGYn3tFbMWRHA17k_qOaVb_8ZaTIRWrbKrYc20ken-Mt1-YuXJEfg/4h4/as2di-Z4RFe2kG8NusbAcw/h0/h001.zSVwXTkxwy3IJMBX_7pboK2LGyqFNiNZlJ51oryEUE4)

Peter, Trump and Elon's friendship is officially over, _who could have seen this coming?_ The _very_ public breakup went absolutely viral yesterday, with the President of the United States and the richest man in the world trading barbs over social media like highschoolers. Trump tweeted he wanted to end all of Elon's government contracts. In response, Elon shared the least shocking news of all time - that the Jeffrey Epstein files were being withheld because Trump was in them.
---
| [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-0kKzB4QgF380jLRJclLJi99vJQbvrEA3tJaNChgWYLS3VBuYVZunOw9XBx_r2UWidMhkfm1ObewcgIDPkEFSi12N44GGMVJWRtjUnSBCMkMD1_84TtxwYu2i9xrONG0kPZyBbZTQ1FtIdLQkZi9H5PSbV2tLzMqi7QCWwk32G79-V73ZPKV0p1kH9at0Wyp-ycKBdkXY3ENekGk3RK5HPnzZwwNRSgV9T71jTbUVa1e_T-vTJCBXoahd2qT4evP8A/4h4/as2di-Z4RFe2kG8NusbAcw/h1/h001.WIsbbaaCXE3luqRvDK1IBLzyTdrckQXrd-CsAOALsvs)
---
**Just like in Wisconsin 's Supreme Court race, Trump was banking on Elon's billions for Pennsylvania's 2025 Supreme Court retention election**. Now he's lost his bff _and a critical funding source_. Trump is trying to take down the three Democratic justices on the PA Supreme Court, with the goal of replacing these judges with loyalists who will ensure PA elections are no longer fair or competitive - turning it deep red. There will be other shady billionaires involved in the race, but **the absence of MAGA 's biggest spender means Defend Our Courts can have an even greater impact on the outcome of this race. ** The breakup is a huge opportunity for Democrats - _with Elon Musk likely on the sidelines, MAGA will no longer have his billions at their disposal._ _ _**Right now, our team is hard at work drafting our first advertisements about the election - aimed at setting the stakes and raising awareness about the rare off-year election. We want to get started fast and take advantage of Elon 's absence.** [**With your help, we can be one of the first ones on the air - setting the tone for the race. The bigger the war chest, the more voters we can reach. Can you rush a donation to help us get our ads on the air and reach as many voters as possible?**](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-0kKzB4QgF380jLRJclLJi8mh3DvAeaC0670AzIvDLB9a_7ork6ZNrT2FWo5LsjEowN4vLwMYs1Fl_PFOTdPrTJVvWrowBPbaOl8zw57VpPZZVV-Rx0MIvATUYKu8XDPeaz9RZFYY09Hs71ZWBCrQGHYx-lAaGB1706n1pI-f4KeX0LDyStwk2CXzmkIPWA2QBsjLcuuyYwr9Wje8l_mbeGBT3NoznvuFtridFvp0HGEHWZHzLYUTslSm7funa4KdA/4h4/as2di-Z4RFe2kG8NusbAcw/h2/h001.7AF1BnRsB5VJ1O8ZGlYJXNwih-ZogtQuEfDZ3X7cZ5k)

clean_body: Their blowup is a huge opportunity for Democrats in PA - with Elon Musk likely on the sidelines, MAGA will no longer have Elon’s billions at their disposal. | | Peter, Trump and Elon's friendship is officially over, who could have seen this coming? The very public breakup went absolutely viral yesterday, with the President of the United States and the richest man in the world trading barbs over social media like highschoolers. Trump tweeted he wanted to end all of Elon's government contracts. In response, Elon shared the least shocking news of all time - that the Jeffrey Epstein files were being withheld because Trump was in them. | Just like in Wisconsin 's Supreme Court race, Trump was banking on Elon's billions for Pennsylvania's 2025 Supreme Court retention election. Now he's lost his bff and a critical funding source. Trump is trying to take down the three Democratic justices on the PA Supreme Court, with the goal of replacing these judges with loyalists who will ensure PA elections are no longer fair or competitive - turning it deep red. There will be other shady billionaires involved in the race, but the absence of MAGA 's biggest spender means Defend Our Courts can have an even greater impact on the outcome of this race. The breakup is a huge opportunity for Democrats - with Elon Musk likely on the sidelines, MAGA will no longer have his billions at their disposal. Right now, our team is hard at work drafting our first advertisements about the election - aimed at setting the stakes and raising awareness about the rare off-year election. We want to get started fast and take advantage of Elon 's absence. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Now is the time to strike. Team Defend our Courts | Mail checks to: Defend Our Courts P.O. Box 45569 Philadelphia, PA 19149 | | | Sent via . name: EMILYs List
subject: Sen. Joni Ernst said the quiet part out loud...
body:
She admitted what we’ve known all along: GOP cuts come with a body count. |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109110891/550034671/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE613_20250606_ErnstTownHallRS_ALL&sourceid=1056360&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYjhjMjgzNS1mYjQyLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PptqgiprVZyFkhZ2mjZmH678fFpOwfRLgd9dGp0qGhA=) Last week, GOP Sen. Joni Ernst said the quiet part out loud at a town hall.

When a concerned constituent pointed out that people would die as a result of Republicans' devastating proposed cuts to Medicaid, Ernst's response wasn't concern. It wasn't urgency. It wasn't compassion. It was a shrug, a smirk — and six chilling words:

**"We all are going to die."**

This is the Republican agenda in action: Prioritize billionaire tax breaks above all. Cut off lifesaving medical care from the people who need it most. Slash services families rely on — and then brush off the human cost.

At EMILYs List, we're sounding the alarm. It's still early in the cycle, but comments like this are a clear signal to us: It's time to take a closer look at states like Iowa and races like Joni Ernst's (who happens to be up for election in 2026!). We've seen this kind of cruel nonchalance from Republicans since the day Trump arrived at the White House. Comments like Ernst's don't just expose how dangerous the Republican agenda is — they also create real political vulnerability. The public outrage in response to Ernst's remarks has proven one thing: Voters across the political spectrum are paying attention, and they're furious.

If Republicans are willing to be this brazenly dismissive and careless to their own voters just to stay in Donald Trump's good graces, it won't be long until the ripple effect reaches the ballot box. **In fact, they may be more beatable than anyone thought.**

clean_body: She admitted what we’ve known all along: GOP cuts come with a body count. | = Last week, GOP Sen. Joni Ernst said the quiet part out loud at a town hall. When a concerned constituent pointed out that people would die as a result of Republicans' devastating proposed cuts to Medicaid, Ernst's response wasn't concern. It wasn't urgency. It wasn't compassion. It was a shrug, a smirk — and six chilling words: "We all are going to die." This is the Republican agenda in action: Prioritize billionaire tax breaks above all. Cut off lifesaving medical care from the people who need it most. Slash services families rely on — and then brush off the human cost. At EMILYs List, we're sounding the alarm. It's still early in the cycle, but comments like this are a clear signal to us: It's time to take a closer look at states like Iowa and races like Joni Ernst's (who happens to be up for election in 2026!). We've seen this kind of cruel nonchalance from Republicans since the day Trump arrived at the White House. Comments like Ernst's don't just expose how dangerous the Republican agenda is — they also create real political vulnerability. The public outrage in response to Ernst's remarks has proven one thing: Voters across the political spectrum are paying attention, and they're furious. If Republicans are willing to be this brazenly dismissive and careless to their own voters just to stay in Donald Trump's good graces, it won't be long until the ripple effect reaches the ballot box. In fact, they may be more beatable than anyone thought. **=** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = We're not exaggerating when we say: Republican policies have a body count. And if we let these candidates go unchallenged, it will only get worse. Thanks for stepping up, the EMILYs List team = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: George Latimer
subject: Can you sign on?

body:
Together we can end gun violence

George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/-Phg2RhDIEGJVPbb5vK87g2?unsubscribedata=kWY7ul4WB%2BurbtUnYAMxhBxOHXJsDNsciMX7oIz8zjiZTY53%2FiI7WiFqFhvZ13M%2FqEbifrNoppz6sPgXFl%2FwC6txtGz4BIhGxoP9xCkaMDPNZJRY7BoPYaN6aY6BcHJq2vwK12Od0n6K1UySGfNSCljsEYH%2Bd%2FdXnuAruXQMomGcoAdGS0KwFXGs3HwP%2FKM%2BlX%2FG62E818%2FQYa7LuiF4ZmeCQfqQjKxLLfvGb1ETw3csVg%2FvEpVDc3TXjtwVn6L5XiH4ECZvHmn2AWBpDliEc5UjfFQZq0Dqy1pC1MSUCAVU0kE7GQoChYDLogynGxnk5U09N8LZCVrjiKS7YVtkqrurtlFcbr92DpGtU018rDiKgOzE%2BbJBh60%2Fly%2F4ms2wTntQxqDAn6ASZIn%2FYtX2q46wyuuHEDbhxpLh1n0DJXurHr5G%2FPvOT4K4qhMvo%2BDg)

Peter,

Today I’m wearing orange for Gun Violence Awareness Day, and I wanted to take a moment to reaffirm my commitment to creating a safer America.

This public health crisis destroys lives, invokes tragedy into our communities, and threatens our nation. That’s why we need smart, common-sense legislation to **end gun violence once and for all.**

[Americans should never have to live under the threat of gun violence. subject: Tim Walz calls ICE agents the Gestapo

body:
I’m a former Navy SEAL running for the U.S. Senate, and I believe in immigration enforcement and the deportation of criminal illegal aliens. That does not make me a Nazi. | [](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c145c7d966641f691c2141402dc7c5d&_e=1JZYvdEpyO6vs0YqiR1Ixli9NfhvuOwyHDGlpebhCNyY9xDUrb9zhWLQrDtBvbYhmEKUiC9_pZuc7TskRRU6GGJ_PK_DT5x9LEB3B65bzgeqZIQUsgaAFqIUgDoeKEamik5ZUHeuBfoMlNLyGuGl_P8QA_7mzONHcbQOhPzoQEX5RvTzWuGKGWgoU54nyE_xA_yPYppCYFtUpIVWCdHcpwvVzsInD_BXgv6rlUDSfkdQ_LK28fQwJFWhPiJLwPOW8bkcTyxJRvZ5r7w344D7iub4H1lcQQXSLofuvidLjwUDaggiEV1tpKEuFgrcPR8b_2ofNWhpFrhpaFttz2voCRuRUQHj6I_X2Co1tm3exX7lDRLqFy1Mz0Cng4LxiW5XYdqvj-oKwy6pHD6Q6fIPnw%3D%3D)

Fellow Conservative,

My name is Adam Schwarze, and if this is your first time hearing from me, let me introduce myself.

I was born in St. Paul, Minnesota, in 1983 and adopted by Joel and Deb Schwarze when I was six months old. I grew up in Bloomington, where hockey was more than a pastime—it was a foundation for leadership and teamwork.

After the terrorist attacks on 9/11, I felt a deep calling to serve. **On my 18th birthday in October 2001, I enlisted as an infantryman in the United States Marine Corps. I deployed with the 1st Marine Division during Operation Iraqi Freedom, earning multiple valor and meritorious promotions awards.**

In 2013, I achieved my lifelong ambition by becoming a **Navy SEAL Officer, serving with SEAL Team Three and later joining the elite undersea-focused enterprise of U.S. Special Operations Command.** Over my 21-year military career, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, conducting combat, intelligence, and humanitarian missions.

Now, I'm stepping up to serve Minnesota in a new capacity—as a Republican candidate for U.S. Senate.

**The Stakes in This Race Are Enormous.**

I'm running against **Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor** , who is even more radical than he is. While Minneapolis was burning during the 2020 riots, she wasn't defending the people or supporting the police—she was preparing a lawsuit _**against**_ the Minneapolis Police Department.

Governor Walz wants America to believe **Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents are the Gestapo.** His words, not mine. And this is who they call a “moderate.”

So ask yourself: _**If this is what their moderates sound like, what is their real agenda?**_

**Every time the left doesn't get their way, they call Republicans Nazis.**

Every time we demand border security or want criminal illegal aliens deported, they call us bigots.

But you know better—and so do I. Enforcing immigration law is not hateful.**It's common sense**. It's about **protecting Americans**.

Fellow Conservative, if you're sick of being called a Nazi because you want a secure border, then **[I’m asking you to pitch in $1, $5, or even $15 today.](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c145c7d966641f691c2141402dc7c5d&_e=1JZYvdEpyO6vs0YqiR1Ixli9NfhvuOwyHDGlpebhCNyY9xDUrb9zhWLQrDtBvbYhmEKUiC9_pZuc7TskRRU6GGJ_PK_DT5x9LEB3B65bzgeqZIQUsgaAFqIUgDoeKEamik5ZUHeuBfoMlNLyGuGl_P8QA_7mzONHcbQOhPzoQEX5RvTzWuGKGWgoU54nyE_xA_yPYppCYFtUpIVWCdHcpwvVzsInD_BXgv6rlUDSfkdQ_LK28fQwJFWhPiJLwPOW3kVC_xINssiuMlMR4fvDcScC8Wi72GZjDFmcaOfHcvXCbwQBAPOBcwLrv18k1X8SnsvFcBVCYOgf6quJI3n3r-7CfjsNGCkkkU8bNXCOwhFbi-ij0Ikpk8762BdZy-l8fcPtUepLnt0PpQaaeykRCA%3D%3D)**

[**SEND ADAM TO THE SENATE**](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c145c7d966641f691c2141402dc7c5d&_e=1JZYvdEpyO6vs0YqiR1Ixli9NfhvuOwyHDGlpebhCNyY9xDUrb9zhWLQrDtBvbYhmEKUiC9_pZuc7TskRRU6GGJ_PK_DT5x9LEB3B65bzgeqZIQUsgaAFqIUgDoeKEamik5ZUHeuBfoMlNLyGuGl_P8QA_7mzONHcbQOhPzoQEX5RvTzWuGKGWgoU54nyE_xA_yPYppCYFtUpIVWCdHcpwvVzsInD_BXgv6rlUDSfkdQ_LK28fQwJFWhPiJLwPOWceSp1PcYbVWtB-DkfLblEwkEltrbX8D9CzG0cuCgeWoMwUjuJPTf1Dzd14JXOyMnwaGTaRW5OE_Diy3uNmRuAqFOGAhui9mKMFjzimMsxSjPSyfIL1cz2ScAMcrGoODU5DaOk6jiKslXtH4cl8Wi5w%3D%3D) **_
_**
[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c145c7d966641f691c2141402dc7c5d&_e=1JZYvdEpyO6vs0YqiR1Ixli9NfhvuOwyHDGlpebhCNyY9xDUrb9zhWLQrDtBvbYhmEKUiC9_pZuc7TskRRU6GGJ_PK_DT5x9LEB3B65bzgeqZIQUsgaAFqIUgDoeKEamik5ZUHeuBfoMlNLyGuGl_P8QA_7mzONHcbQOhPzoQEX5RvTzWuGKGWgoU54nyE_xA_yPYppCYFtUpIVWCdHcpwvVzsInD_BXgv6rlUDSfkdQ_LK28fQwJFWhPiJLwPOWg4bfCSN_Un6ZJj5EGJCB13eYz01LwbAILLPBv1luEzkgFrBRRYCLi7BQbuSWnFD1TamKC4CC8GJxaC1St_qt33NT5UuWlZ3kPOfiDswLmKd1PGEH-Q0fI4Ye6oxsuj-9tadDqgfUar1Qp-JTJCdKqA%3D%3D)

**Here's What I Believe:** I believe that no one is above the law—especially criminal illegal aliens who commit rape, murder, and violence against American citizens.

I believe in a **strong border, deporting those who break our laws, and respecting the rule of law** —no exceptions, no apologies.

I believe that veterans, police officers, working families, and law-abiding Americans deserve a voice in the Senate—not professional politicians who mock us and ignore our communities.

**I've Never Run for Office Before. That's the Point.**

Unlike my opponent, I wasn't groomed for politics. I joined the military on my 18th birthday—October 2001—just weeks after the attacks on our homeland. I didn't run from duty. I ran toward it.

While Tim Walz was dodging deployments, I was deployed nine times. I fought to protect conservative American values—the ones that raised me, the ones that made this country great, and the ones that are now under daily assault.

Tim Walz hand-picked my opponent in the U.S. Senate race to be his lieutenant governor. We can’t trust him due to his past actions in the military and the governor's mansion, so it should go without saying that we can’t trust her, either.

Fellow Conservative, I'm asking you for something personal today—something I never thought I'd do: **[Could you donate $10, $25, or even $50 to help me launch this campaign the right way?](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c145c7d966641f691c2141402dc7c5d&_e=1JZYvdEpyO6vs0YqiR1Ixli9NfhvuOwyHDGlpebhCNyY9xDUrb9zhWLQrDtBvbYhmEKUiC9_pZuc7TskRRU6GGJ_PK_DT5x9LEB3B65bzgeqZIQUsgaAFqIUgDoeKEamik5ZUHeuBfoMlNLyGuGl_P8QA_7mzONHcbQOhPzoQEX5RvTzWuGKGWgoU54nyE_xA_yPYppCYFtUpIVWCdHcpwvVzsInD_BXgv6rlUDSfkdQ_LK28fQwJFWhPiJLwPOW4wP_7dGDm_CPenrpDkiaof96WQIRSruy5BEzXN4tgzc-t0HcZQn0DinbsrgBoPevyMnc3YTxYR2ytKkHxC5-_wI8oiD40f6UQbmuR2g935el8L4aQSWZSnT_XzlnQVo74vKFQCiqlEq1K86ie02FYw%3D%3D)**

[**HELP ME GET MY CAMPAIGN OFF THE GROUND WITH A GIFT OF $5 OR MORE**](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c145c7d966641f691c2141402dc7c5d&_e=1JZYvdEpyO6vs0YqiR1Ixli9NfhvuOwyHDGlpebhCNyY9xDUrb9zhWLQrDtBvbYhmEKUiC9_pZuc7TskRRU6GGJ_PK_DT5x9LEB3B65bzgeqZIQUsgaAFqIUgDoeKEamik5ZUHeuBfoMlNLyGuGl_P8QA_7mzONHcbQOhPzoQEX5RvTzWuGKGWgoU54nyE_xA_yPYppCYFtUpIVWCdHcpwvVzsInD_BXgv6rlUDSfkdQ_LK28fQwJFWhPiJLwPOWiKkkHxL7fYm8Dur4EFJDN47SW-xtCq4zBjlAi3_4OFwINwmBUZ6b8uqnOqH_2YQ7MZxsJvJeqfQd3UFlxe8cZ_jolZ0bQDxOQNN5v59HKrzpPC64cIQ-DZYQRPOY_sQ2J8M_hDAgvjcXReF9yL8UCA%3D%3D)
Minnesota hasn't gone Republican in a presidential race since 1976. But we're not a deep-blue state. Not yet.

With Senator Tina Smith retiring, this is our moment. The seat is open. The field is fluid. And I believe—with everything in me—that if we build now, we can flip this seat, and flip the narrative.

**I Don't Have the Media. I Don't Have Hollywood. I Don't Have the Swamp.**

What I have is grit, **a record of service** , and hopefully, the support of **patriots like you** who still believe America is worth fighting for. If you're tired of watching cities like Minneapolis fall apart while liberal elites cheer it on...

And if you believe we deserve **leaders who've fought for this country—** Then I'm humbly asking you to join my team today.

[**CHIP IN TO SEND A NAVY SEAL TO THE SENATE**](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c145c7d966641f691c2141402dc7c5d&_e=1JZYvdEpyO6vs0YqiR1Ixli9NfhvuOwyHDGlpebhCNyY9xDUrb9zhWLQrDtBvbYhmEKUiC9_pZuc7TskRRU6GGJ_PK_DT5x9LEB3B65bzgeqZIQUsgaAFqIUgDoeKEamik5ZUHeuBfoMlNLyGuGl_P8QA_7mzONHcbQOhPzoQEX5RvTzWuGKGWgoU54nyE_xA_yPYppCYFtUpIVWCdHcpwvVzsInD_BXgv6rlUDSfkdQ_LK28fQwJFWhPiJLwPOWn1gpn90uUW5MQP7Xva32guzgAynkkePjbapXkobG0iKiB4R1SmnIppU4hLDHPk5pRAtt0SsHxEhFs7fn5M5-FnsveAedqaXJ1rw22ldP1tBKVpHZoI762iUsd4EL5QR56anppAAEoAXvM_fFwMfxbQ%3D%3D)
[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c145c7d966641f691c2141402dc7c5d&_e=1JZYvdEpyO6vs0YqiR1Ixli9NfhvuOwyHDGlpebhCNyY9xDUrb9zhWLQrDtBvbYhmEKUiC9_pZuc7TskRRU6GGJ_PK_DT5x9LEB3B65bzgeqZIQUsgaAFqIUgDoeKEamik5ZUHeuBfoMlNLyGuGl_P8QA_7mzONHcbQOhPzoQEX5RvTzWuGKGWgoU54nyE_xA_yPYppCYFtUpIVWCdHcpwvVzsInD_BXgv6rlUDSfkdQ_LK28fQwJFWhPiJLwPOWz1c5iSzXblBpoHfo1iCGX0WRHsL0rK0YITqm_UOwHjTBwR8Dah_I4lBl5dQo5L2cCCzu4zgaTO7wM2gesqz0yNBWRJPHMPOS2l2ATOYBvuoZoiWHqVIdoduS_W26YLE7vOl1Y1njFt9u7pZ20iXf2Q%3D%3D)

Let's do this. Let's turn the tide. Let's win.

With gratitude,
Adam Schwarze
Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota

And I believe—with everything in me—that if we build now, we can flip this seat, and flip the narrative. I Don't Have the Media. I Don't Have Hollywood. I Don't Have the Swamp. What I have is grit, a record of service , and hopefully, the support of patriots like you who still believe America is worth fighting for. If you're tired of watching cities like Minneapolis fall apart while liberal elites cheer it on... And if you believe we deserve leaders who've fought for this country— Then I'm humbly asking you to join my team today. Let's do this. Let's turn the tide. Let's win. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

clean_body: | ~ | Peter, I’m so done with Trump and Elon’s BS. ~ It’s only been a few days since Elon officially left the White House (with a trail of destruction in his wake), and he and Trump are already in petty squabbles all over the internet. Let’s call this ego-contest what it is, Peter: A distraction. A distraction from the fact that these two men have crafted policy, executive orders, and bills designed to help their billionaire cronies, oppress minorities, and destroy the working-class. Just yesterday, Elon called the “Big Beautiful Bill” a disgrace – not because it hurts working families, because it might hurt his own personal profit. But while Trump and Elon are throwing hissy fits on their respective social media propaganda platforms, I’m focusing on fighting for the working people who are suffering from their policies. I’ll never stop calling out Trump and Elon on their BS, but I can’t do it alone. ~ If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | ~~ Let’s get to actual work, Maxwell Frost | ~ Maxwell Alejandro Frost is an organizer representing Florida's 10th Congressional District. name: Team Benson
subject: How Jocelyn will deliver for Michigan
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)
Jocelyn is running for governor with one goal in mind, folks: to make Michigan the best place to be a kid, raise a kid, and call home.
Since this campaign is heating up (_we’re already 6 days into June!_), we wanted to reach out today to share with you 6 ways Jocelyn will deliver for Michigan. But if you’ve already heard enough, [**click here to make a donation and help elect Jocelyn as governor.**](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/854?c0=jmb-em-q22025.35DAUP&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)
1. **Jocelyn is fighting to ensure that our government works for everyone.** She understands that if we want Michigan to be a place where everyone can prosper, leaders need to put saving time and money at the forefront, ensure access to affordable health care and housing, and implement bold, innovative new ideas to make real progress for Michiganders.

2. **Jocelyn will make sure child care is affordable and Michigan is truly the best place to raise a kid. Raising a family shouldn’t be financially out of reach.** She’ll tackle Michigan’s childcare crisis by investing in workers, expanding access, and making care available for every parent or guardian — especially in rural and underserved areas.

3. **Jocelyn believes that the government works best when our rights are protected and every person is respected.** As governor, she will stand up against anyone to make sure we are safe and protected — regardless of who we love, who we worship, or where in the state we call home.

4. **Jocelyn knows that a strong education system is the foundation for a strong future in Michigan.** As the daughter of two school teachers, she saw the importance of public education firsthand. There’s still more work to do to take us to the top ten in public education, which is why she will work to cut red tape and fully fund our public schools.

5. **Jocelyn understands the impact that rising costs have on Michiganders.** With a people-driven economic agenda, she will fight to build more housing, lower the cost of health care, and protect small businesses from Trump’s reckless tariffs.

6. **Jocelyn is laser-focused on ensuring elections in Michigan remain free, fair, and transparent.** Jocelyn oversaw the highest turnout and most secure elections in Michigan’s history by making it easier to vote and harder to cheat, but our voting rights are still under attack. That’s why, as governor, she will continue to ensure every Michigander can have confidence in their voice and their vote.

[**So what do you say? With those reasons in mind, can you chip in $6 to Jocelyn’s campaign today to help her win this race and fight for a stronger Michigan?**](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/1997?c0=jmb-em-q22025.t8rNCH&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
| [CHIP IN $6 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/1998?c0=jmb-em-q22025.OQxXEZ&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)
---
[CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/263?c0=jmb-em-q22025.lB2dD6&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)
---
| [CHIP IN $25 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/264?c0=jmb-em-q22025.a7vAC6&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&t=6&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)
---
[CHIP IN $50 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/265?c0=jmb-em-q22025.QdisXL&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&t=7&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)
---
| [CHIP IN $100 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/266?c0=jmb-em-q22025.YpMn4N&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&t=8&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)
---
[ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q22025.rGq0NW&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&t=9&akid=388%2E181023%2EvBffxQ)

Thank you!
— Team Benson
