name: New PA Project Education Fund
subject: Civic Education is more important than ever
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
<a clicktracking=off href="http://example.com">
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
Earlier this week, the PA [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] House State Government Committee [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house.
An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills.
HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.”
State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [https://www.politicspa.com/state-government-committee-sends-voter-id-election-omnibus-bills-to-house-floor/141399] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.”
HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.”
Among other changes, [https://penncapital-star.com/campaigns-elections/voter-id-and-vote-by-mail-reform-bills-move-to-pa-house] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers.
The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed.
The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error.
Upcoming events
As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Pittsburgh%20School%20board%20forum%208.5x11.png]
Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students.
Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader.
With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color.
When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793662/542679746/-435660973]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Allegheny%20judicial%20flyer.png]
Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot.
Your vote shapes our justice system!
Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager
Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms.
When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793661/542679745/229023784]
Social Media of the Week
https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/ [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM.
Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa]
Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th.
We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location.
#GoVotePA
SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/7237344C447445787878394747342B75584E47375A57552F745265427662334B544A4A36505A58657A5871474B7A5A6E53705571424758325657365969365747/editor_images/WCM%20logo.JPG] #CourtsMatter
This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/a-double-win-for-voters-in-pennsylvania] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls.
First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/445-2025-04-24-Order-denying-PA-Attorney-Generals-motion-to-intervene.pdf] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots.
The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/cases/pennsylvania-undated-and-wrongly-dated-mail-in-ballots-challenge-eakin/] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted.
After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/441-2025-04-02-Notice-Of-Appeal-.pdf] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary.
Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as Citizen_AG, filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/1-2024-10-29-Complaint.pdf] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act.
The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls.
District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/67-2025-05-02-Memorandum-.pdf] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place.
In law, standing [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standing_(law)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case.
In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money]
The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests.
In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money] .
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/DelCo_2.png] We are hiring!
Let's provide civic education together!
We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania.
Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote!
Positions start at $20 an hour.
Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org]
EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org]
News You Can Use
There are things every one of us can do [https://www.democracydocket.com/opinion/we-have-more-power-than-trump-wants-us-to-believe-heres-what-you-can-do/] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last.
1. Educate Yourself
Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally.
Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more.
* New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pittsburgh-mayor-election-2025-candidates-quiz]
* Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections/assistant/]
* Everything you need to know about mail voting [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-mail-ballot-how-to-request-fill-out-return] , becoming a poll worker [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide]
* Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pennsylvania-primary-election-day-2025-voter-information-registration]
* Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-commonwealth-superior-court-candidates] , vetting judicial candidates [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-candidate-vetting-guide]
* Guides to State College school board [https://www.spotlightpa.org/statecollege/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-state-college-school-board-anderson-huff-barlow-black-desmarais-mcternan-sogor] , Reading City Council president [https://www.spotlightpa.org/berks/2025/04/reading-city-council-president-primary-election-2025]
* Guides to voting as a college student [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-college-student-voting-guide]
En Español
* Guía para votar por correo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pensilvania-elecciones-primarias-2025-votar-correo-como-llenar] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/reading-concejo-municipal-primarias-elecciones-2025]
* Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-primarias-elecciones-2025-votar-informacion] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-elecciones-2025-como-convertirse-trabajador-electoral]
2. Stay Engaged
To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win.
Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/pennsylvania/montgomery-county-commissioner-neil-makhija-emmy-20250508.html] )
3. Share What You Learn
Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting.
Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year!
4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations
Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work.
Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above!
5. Become a Trained Poll Worker
Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process.
Here are some resources that may be helpful:
Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [https://penncapital-star.com/briefs/advocacy-groups-offer-training-for-pa-primary-poll-watchers/]
Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide/]
Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [https://fcfreepresspa.com/poll-workers-needed-for-pennsylvanias-2025-primary-election/]
6. Engage Your Elected Officials
Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise.
7. Vote in Every Election
Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility.
Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th!
Recent Events

On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights.
We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change.
This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division.

clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! Earlier this week, the PA [] House State Government Committee [] [] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house. An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills. HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.” State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.” HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.” Among other changes, [] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers. The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed. The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error. Upcoming events As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls. [] Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students. Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader. With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color. When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] [] Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot. Your vote shapes our justice system! Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms. When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] Social Media of the Week [] The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM. Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa] Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th. We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location. #GoVotePA SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [] [] #CourtsMatter This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls. First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots. The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted. After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary. Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as CitizenAG, filed [] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act. The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls. District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place. In law, standing [)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case. In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [] The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests. In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [] . [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org] EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use There are things every one of us can do [] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last. 1. Educate Yourself Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally. Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more. Stay Engaged To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win. Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [] ) 3. Today, you're receiving Hillsdale College's **Second Amendment Survey** because you're someone who understands that education is essential to the defense of liberty. Your timely survey answers will help us reach and teach millions of our fellow citizens in the coming year about the importance of liberty--which includes the rights protected by the Second Amendment. There is no question that one of the Left's most powerful tools in its ongoing effort to undermine our liberty is **ignorance**. Here at Hillsdale, we have been pursuing truth and defending liberty since the College was founded in 1844. Today, our educational outreach efforts include programs on a variety of topics essential to liberty, such as the Second Amendment. As constitutional scholar Edward J. Erler observed in _Imprimis_ , our digest of liberty that reaches millions of Americans: "The Second Amendment is an expression of **first principles**." This means the Second Amendment is essential for the preservation of freedom. If we are denied the means of defending our freedom, we will eventually lose our freedom. **But instead of just TALKING, it 's more important to DO something about it.** That's why we have been working to expand our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty. And this work continues today. This is why we **reach** and **teach** millions of Americans about the principles of liberty and the Constitution. It's also why we need your help today. As I mentioned, Hillsdale refuses to accept ONE PENNY of government funding--not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans. Our independence from the government means that our work is entirely dependent on the generous support of citizens like you, who understand the importance of education to liberty. The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. subject: Foxes in the hen house

body:
DemDocket: DOJ’s New Top Voting Lawyer Worked for Leading Anti-Voting Law Firm| | | We wish there was a hero riding over the hill at full gallop to come save us, folks. Real life isn't the next binge-worthy series on TV, however, and it's up to us, our movement, and our supporters to fight back when we let the literal foxes into the hen house with vote-suppressors running the Department of Justice's Civil Rights division. **[Our fundamental right to vote is the most precious resource our democracy has. Please, RUSH $25 or more today to help us defend it in every state across the country>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109113161/550042951/1723810624?am=25.00&contactdata=Lg+t2d8SDfXiHlpKFf1GWEGMzv3PR6xn0Ju/nWAYjeBx+lB7a8cTxKNB4YsSatejmL3brToRCG5nRZ7hVUwCoBadti09DYOFNCrnKYyX4dc=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBhY2U2MjU1LTAwNDMtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=gsXv_o06Zlg8LcImZV6OygCnivKXsp4K3-IBe6q4ZEs=&emci=553a7e16-ed42-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=0ace6255-0043-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=33603084)**

---
This seems like something out of a twisted novel, folks, but **the new top voting lawyer at the Department of Justice was until recently an attorney and activist for a leading anti-voting legal group** that has worked for years to spread fear about unproven illegal voting and press election officials to implement stricter, suppressive voting rules. The lawyer, Maureen Riordan, worked side-by-side with Cleta Mitchell — the right-wing activist who played a key role in President Donald Trump’s failed bid to subvert the results of the 2020 election — following Mitchell’s pledge to “ _reclaim our election systems from the left._ ” This appointment, along with the posting of Trump campaign lawyer and election denier Harmeet Dhillon as head of the Civil Rights division, shows how DOJ has gone from a largely consistent defender of voting rights to instead working actively to undermine our fundamental rights. **Again, are we surprised? No.** But these moves from the administration highlight how badly **we need to fight for our fundamental rights as citizens** , from the outside of government. **[Please, chip in $25 or whatever you can today to help us defend the right to vote across the country, from state legislatures to courtrooms to the ballot box>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109113166/550042975/1723810624?am=25.00&contactdata=Lg+t2d8SDfXiHlpKFf1GWEGMzv3PR6xn0Ju/nWAYjeBx+lB7a8cTxKNB4YsSatejmL3brToRCG5nRZ7hVUwCoBadti09DYOFNCrnKYyX4dc=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBhY2U2MjU1LTAwNDMtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=gsXv_o06Zlg8LcImZV6OygCnivKXsp4K3-IBe6q4ZEs=&emci=553a7e16-ed42-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=0ace6255-0043-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=33603084)**

| | Riordan's first lawsuit at her new job? Filed recently against North Carolina, it cites Trump’s March anti-voter executive order and aims to strip legitimate voters from the rolls and force North Carolina to do more to collect unnecessary information that’s required by new, suppressive state laws, all from people newly registering to vote. This follows her work at the harmless-sounding Public Interest Legal Foundation (PILF), which is anything but. PILF specializes in pressuring states and counties — _by threatening and bringing expensive and frivolous lawsuits_ — to more aggressively remove voters from the rolls. As part of that effort, PILF has worked to stoke fear about the threat of non-citizen voting, which is extremely rare. Remember, the civil rights division of DOJ was created in 1957 -- often referred to as the “crown jewel” of the department -- has always been a cornerstone in the fight to protect our right to vote, not suing to restrict it or cut off access for voters seeking to exercise their rights. But Trump's nominees to DOJ have**sent out “mission statements” that made it clear the civil rights division was shifting its focus from protecting the civil rights of marginalized people to supporting Trump’s priorities and anti-voting conspiracy theories.** This administration is seemingly going to do everything in their power to attack our right to vote until they ensconce their control forever. name: Sen. Tim Sheehy
subject: No stranger to tough fights
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA
body:
Washington’s a different kind of warzone—high stakes, relentless battles, and no shortage of enemies. Fellow Conservative,

Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one’s shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway.

The out-of-touch fake news “reporters” at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs.

We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can’t abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country.

_**Yet the liberal news corporations, who _desperately_ want to keep the status quo, _attacked us_ for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government.**_

All of us –**Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke** – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we’re up against more than just the DC establishment. We’re up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats.

**We need you, Fellow Conservative to keep us on the frontlines, bucking the corrupt status quo! **[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=4ee1555ae2914f2d9ac1be60053c84b6&_e=CUG1rjL4pxrUfBZw8ASvMLU3MR59CiXLT0QfKNRztu28KEEioeWzcetTboKS40nWX9VT8MIAH7TY3_XLqWfUBacb64Yo4B-eURPTYr7x9irEXBcTKHXraL7cBPTzpWVuN2MNVovHqG2gbuXDhInCno3iOfpxtdcJardkjsTp10kEOgAee93YeKTPv7z5ecKgbuorszzgvu8_kc8nppoR20MvMRxYTQ_aplGzBHHP5Wj6UQAsvF79t61QR7YzwZ3hHtxq3UQ24TJeyqsEyaJ2DD3CdcZuP9rhJpP3DmJbczUBHQYdo3cLIBIxHygJtN9E6XzEwZ2vVyBSLRmgo7JNzEkf9UDjc4DABTXT5bzjzR1Z4O9DWCXyqANvCqrrQrwHx8gH07pANeb3KlFlYGf2ZA%3D%3D)

→ If the media wants to attack us for having a “warrior mentality” – the mentality to do whatever it takes to achieve a mission...

→ If the media wants to attack us for supporting President Trump’s MAGA movement – the movement that says we can make America great...

→ If the media wants to attack us for taking on the government corruption, fraud, and waste – which has been exposed by Trump’s DOGE department...

_**...Then they don’t understand WHY Donald J. Trump and ALL of us were elected!**_

As SEALs, we all shoot straight with each other. We’re going to shoot straight with you, too: **This hit piece was horsesh*t and part of a larger conspiracy to silence us.**

|

Friend, I'm Daniel Hernandez, and I'm announcing my Democratic Campaign for the Arizona Special Election because we need more leaders who will stand up and do whatever it takes to stop MAGA extremists. [**Before I tell you a bit about myself, will you split a $10 donation between my campaign and Ohio House Dems and help us get one step closer to retaking the House?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109113940/550045958/-1593982674?refcode=250606_Hernandez_Running&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYjZmMmEyOS0wMjQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4vZxgnLPlPcW7YMxcOCHnrShJvcyPay4H2eQbf4540Q=) | [**RUSH $10 »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109113941/550045959/1943465842?amount=10&refcode=250606_Hernandez_Running&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYjZmMmEyOS0wMjQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4vZxgnLPlPcW7YMxcOCHnrShJvcyPay4H2eQbf4540Q= "https://secure.actblue.com/")
---
Folks, I was raised to work hard and take care of my community.
Growing up, my family struggled to make ends meet. My mom immigrated from Mexico, and my dad worked double shifts as a construction worker. We depended on Medicaid, and when I lost my health insurance as a teenager, it nearly killed me.
**As the first in my family to attend college, I interned for then-Congresswoman Gabby Giffords. As a former nursing assistant, I was there to administer life-saving aid when she was shot in an act of political violence.**
That day changed my life forever. I witnessed firsthand the devastating effects of gun violence and vowed to use my voice and platform to prevent it.
_These hardships could have ended my career in public service before it even started, but instead, they fueled me to fight._
As a state legislator, I co-founded the LGBTQ caucus and fought to pass bills to protect gay marriage and survivors of sexual assault, increase gun violence prevention resources, and expand access to affordable healthcare.
**So I know a thing or two about getting sh*t done.**
Since gaining their MAGA trifecta, Republicans have done nothing but gut funding for essential programs like the Department of Education to line billionaires' pockets.
I'm running for Congress because I won't sit on the sidelines while they try to strip us of our rights and undermine our democracy.
Unlike most campaigns you're hearing from, **my race isn't in a year and a half – it’s just weeks away.** So we have an urgent opportunity to stop MAGA Republicans and stand up for our communities.
[**So friend, I'm asking you: will you rush anything you can to my campaign and Ohio House Dems so Democrats can WIN this critical Special Election and be one step closer to flipping the House Blue? >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109113942/550045960/-1593982674?refcode=250606_Hernandez_Running&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYjZmMmEyOS0wMjQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4vZxgnLPlPcW7YMxcOCHnrShJvcyPay4H2eQbf4540Q=)

clean_body: | Friend, I'm Daniel Hernandez, and I'm announcing my Democratic Campaign for the Arizona Special Election because we need more leaders who will stand up and do whatever it takes to stop MAGA extremists. = | = "" Folks, I was raised to work hard and take care of my community. Growing up, my family struggled to make ends meet. My mom immigrated from Mexico, and my dad worked double shifts as a construction worker. We depended on Medicaid, and when I lost my health insurance as a teenager, it nearly killed me. As the first in my family to attend college, I interned for then-Congresswoman Gabby Giffords. As a former nursing assistant, I was there to administer life-saving aid when she was shot in an act of political violence. That day changed my life forever. I witnessed firsthand the devastating effects of gun violence and vowed to use my voice and platform to prevent it. These hardships could have ended my career in public service before it even started, but instead, they fueled me to fight. As a state legislator, I co-founded the LGBTQ caucus and fought to pass bills to protect gay marriage and survivors of sexual assault, increase gun violence prevention resources, and expand access to affordable healthcare. So I know a thing or two about getting sht done. Since gaining their MAGA trifecta, Republicans have done nothing but gut funding for essential programs like the Department of Education to line billionaires' pockets. I'm running for Congress because I won't sit on the sidelines while they try to strip us of our rights and undermine our democracy. Unlike most campaigns you're hearing from, my race isn't in a year and a half – it’s just weeks away. So we have an urgent opportunity to stop MAGA Republicans and stand up for our communities. = | = "" | = "" | = "" | = "" Thanks for being in this fight with me, Daniel = = = ** OHDC 545 E. # It's Pride! Don't forget that "Ally" is a Verb
---
Happy Pride! This month, and EVERY month, we stand in love and solidarity with the entire LGBTQIA* community: young, old, and everyone in between. Pride is a time to celebrate, but it's also a time to act. If you consider yourself an ally, now's the moment to show up. [GLAAD reports](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.xqm-apKpWqGuZCDDu9wrxS2aoetpux7LP0ERWucx5u-K7qIUZWW0_9eWXHD8hibOFhvi6K0rmuh63hZaZfVHNJfHOTO9epOYf02CF_fatxQUV2XjtxcrwZa0lGWYj2-DQcsXUGuc4qL4FeH-N0jYZdfKF-vrZsK77zjdr8OWa9H6az_CKNFyHvfAtZU7ejiHevpQk_iTdceJ8M4idBEZpI781oYR79P_F2UB6hCWto4kyvPg2ggIaAnK3EeoNe5kCNwD4iekY8BTr0Ejt_-XgJePeQv2nrGswnArTRifgkGK1QgcHqcanDxEKtZfLLusYze8cogHnT4NN2Wk1TAIIC-rRdm-2uFQPa2KySV31Us/4h4/JJu4oXfUTySPDd7ihJ2Arw/h1/h001.a1YCf2ZBABLSQvTbKQr_uCgCbzvMEJb4EeLnzYqYO1U) there have been at least 255 attacks on LGBTQIA* people through policy and rhetoric since Trump took office. Additionally, Trump and individual states are [fixated on preventing](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMRrCN2mjrcfMvEmS6vtDoCAMcpUQTmg34XwXF-j3iMhOoZQvDl-ANnUDtLtqWbY-SVNQUpMH6vXvfIeMdbIetWNCoEEyKaRl0UbPHIMTC6QxE365EibV55YYB7A8cdZfM-patFn7fyQM9ARzzeLOxVUftSdqVEuc3qmiEkvPN-vq8rSvuzvJotXxen9Aj1KZJGVHTZC8FDXJswj2iE_Ku_yNUSTTQO2Ig86RT6WAVLsCo6h7YoBIyN_-6eX136E1D1W04-IcaF2fLlTLLA4Taqmxllsq1d7E_x9tMmuVfXX7H160jbG2sdVjGwXtNd0vwFJuFQFQD790sVMeiobyNjWmIJngGZAmXyAKvPYrvlegFGa8WxwkOD66VodeOIB_iw/4h4/JJu4oXfUTySPDd7ihJ2Arw/h2/h001.cyWZc_Q4dkfti4uMyEsJdvbcRDOdo3vnSDSVkPsR8To) gender affirming care for minors. While some of these unconstitutional measures have been blocked by the courts, new ones keep coming at an alarming rate, so stay tuned for ways that we can meet the moment together.

# Repro Roundup
---
### A step closer to contraception access for all in PA!
---
Thanks to all of you that showed up, contacted your reps, and spread the word, the PA House of Representatives passed [Pennsylvania House Bill 1140](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMSrJHZoMQrxufM2jpP91IB3Hq1JS5NWnYHX-n88QdazG2lvBQXbWT52aN-1tIPChh5lRKKfMg-_liyswJTWr2bKZHo9jt4IKIwslFO8Gd1xzE6RISisf_a5okQv1k3zTfKxFAj19f4zCt0DGWtCxGbA1ezrtIyy0iUyCNBPrSW7WJoQAevVa4fCcD_vAvAEYvwPLCClpezssYqP9b2qaMEDE5ZQ4ZH5LZHu7phiEo6wD7FeXBq7vkjlk8qCxzfJJ1RJnT9BVGj1EmE-rTpVkNSXQVakEIL-4nZZ5PUwdhJ7mdTAA4SzGv1teO0C69WuV1axNygO7LwI6CacjKMog87etqV3C6mzQ2SMvujrXHock/4h4/JJu4oXfUTySPDd7ihJ2Arw/h3/h001.3iX8QTl3WfW_2MGpXOeKf53UaJnjibmOETA3T0wN_Nc) introduced by Rep. Leanne Krueger. This bill requires insurance companies to cover all contraceptives regardless of income! Now it's on to the PA State Senate and [one step closer to becoming law](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.lmpSTrE-G07FKUPTdpC9IVu9H6aciYCxvxrdHUvkJDsgb_iG_HWzFRSMGYc-FrH4mvjXLMr-NTNqTFrziyY6U0YuqkAaS4fEF5cJx475jIRlXt7gA_-FgpRqRr9XUqywqrCDjYJ5jVJNMtjctuLz2oOTKIcqMfXXAutPNed9d8sV6jewxtsWR3LEoktBe7VI45KnuVvt7dqGxdVmCxHTOfYDb9zBVL1morIpTsACmLG6XAfN5Xmlbch-YeNvVWSgkjpGz2976a9L2mdxwadY0kNDo6MDkDi2ieLKSk1dkEm3xxE74ajzbVAsowE51J2RoMo4lHZGf-GemohyR7u_Aqer6j5H8gz0MyzZPsBt1_j3XLf_i2PzWBoErdyouR2jooyLKvI9OOYRbPyMK9qNN88hxKCFGfPh4gjGelBmcdo9mPOdzN4kW_UrW6FNez4Gl1rlcpYZUqBtcKE7mAcf9NbqteqnXr1vTcY2Vd6m0Yj72rjFF3P2RTGBTW7EYkRD_PvQ-4Spqi1EqTQhyJMQUg/4h4/JJu4oXfUTySPDd7ihJ2Arw/h4/h001.ZhiK91gNSQSyVYl64NMo8WruAvA6tOm1j16hyMWAwUI).

### Trump just made it even more dangerous for pregnant people needing emergency care
---
The Trump administration just rescinded critical guidance that protected pregnant people in emergency rooms needing urgent abortion care. Adding to the confusion, this doesn't change the actual law but removes guidance that was essential for protecting patients in crisis and their doctors, especially in states with abortion bans. This move isn't about health or safety--it's about creating fear and barriers to care. Here's [an overview](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMctRoxlFO9O82fTB5zU1O8GvuZhaYw7gd5YRcOJ92Gf_h0Wfa5G33mEhawvx4XqArgyqjhRWrXiIidgO9W8QAjzrILllLdLyjJtxy8Nne6xkfqLwdlxLrJBv-35ykzSxFedbySlyTy3wQpTT_X_b7v-v9LxoB0lWpuqVs4D0f_gVwv29FvkrYMgxKZYLtqrAxhlgs13C_sqMgEvqbEszKMra3iS8fcvj_YM5Mr0VqYqMYbwpJ1z-o3nxJ_fqAO7vDt7YS0VwqpUgfB0tZ2MA8tKMMrM2Ryzg8-UETuL6OsFPIIyCm9jgMe9GsJgz1DJJz6FaxEvGH2HtlDOU_cr0fAJ6gi4owL0IIyieokcAOwJb/4h4/JJu4oXfUTySPDd7ihJ2Arw/h5/h001.GyvuqHRDlVNPf-djnI6_oxzQn0ApOLyIl_iurAgxeLs) by the Center for Reproductive Rights

### Union Yes! Retaliation No!
---
When the workers at Allegheny Reproductive Health Center informed management of their intent to unionize, staff received no response other than to be denied voluntary union recognition. Since that time, two longtime staff members working to organize the [Allegheny Reproductive Justice Union](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMWn3R7U15hZOprFI75oZg47kLht_3tccVpre3HiFCREjHNsmH4UkhMQus9CETh4c-mSk5LLvIRTZXIFuNSQ8xGofLoHpOe4uwiF2OwS1bjw_qYPqmqBy9Hen3jxef8ACXo8dN8wO32S5ER4ERC-HbqMgE4QTXVw6zIIp50S-NvBsfpek1gTZy6EKi_heq-9dG5yL1fyVRKgkQ_FTTMH-6ysMEs0eZPsj395Ii4pEeQPn90C4Emb7YxNp3ryS2reJzlqnhdCbN5UM0T8-8ijBUFeoaCJjMjhk3RcL4YW_QhQLZ-XXdk2yI-hJ2uJPZrN8zIpMny-2SLe-GGxCwb2DIZE/4h4/JJu4oXfUTySPDd7ihJ2Arw/h7/h001.aZLU0WDzqsa5IUN_IS8aOKhC7ozIMU6_R9Hzc2yC7ks) have been fired in retaliation.This is unacceptable and hugely disappointing behavior from a leadership team that espouses inclusion and safety within their clinic.

# Disability Justice Digest
---
### Transportation
---
KPEF's Disability Justice Director Mallory Hudson and Board Member Alisa Grishman (pictured) joined our colleagues in the [Transit for All PA coalition](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMcsT8RVftT7BCoy0DKdp-j5WV_lnf51fRtBIXHnU_AH6QyWxyyem6A1ghRsL3WdK2s6VX0qBaDKZJoTvjkxGxxpjp1x9SnIlr2NvRGj-6cy_Xd2txCxht6Nxd7CbC8K0AzTjUVhhwqSs-Q2oKDVVcSvtIGBXuK-j7QozIpBU5ysavvox0qgZlWnzG1LLAUqgJ0kBEtcrGxW04cDIW4lAkOrIqFhjGNiuOsIRJiuCqIHwLPesTxgEilw5Ra5EH_ES8xOAwQ8li7UZs5EAT8X8A5seNgLuSsQN9kylz5go23irCln8dms13ExXY3bCgcg0qhieUmFiOwt4zZfxygrKbsE/4h4/JJu4oXfUTySPDd7ihJ2Arw/h8/h001.98oqy_vjbJrMoA0iXcnYTGszxl_6Hib_WhnUiAbIFf0) in Harrisburg this week to tell our lawmakers: **No State Budget Without Transit Funding For All!** In all 67 counties of the Commonwealth, public transportation is a lifeline for our elders, youth, disabled community members, and anyone without access to a personal vehicle. Our coalition has one demand: **Restore transit service across the state to 2019 levels --and expand transit service** in communities **outside of Pittsburgh and Philadelphia** by an additional 10%. Getting our transit service up to 2019 levels in 2025 is achievable and, with your help, our state representatives will act!

clean_body: | | It's Pride! Don't forget that "Ally" is a Verb Happy Pride! This month, and EVERY month, we stand in love and solidarity with the entire LGBTQIA community: young, old, and everyone in between. Pride is a time to celebrate, but it's also a time to act. If you consider yourself an ally, now's the moment to show up. there have been at least 255 attacks on LGBTQIA people through policy and rhetoric since Trump took office. Additionally, Trump and individual states are gender affirming care for minors. While some of these unconstitutional measures have been blocked by the courts, new ones keep coming at an alarming rate, so stay tuned for ways that we can meet the moment together. | Repro Roundup A step closer to contraception access for all in PA! Thanks to all of you that showed up, contacted your reps, and spread the word, the PA House of Representatives passed introduced by Rep. Leanne Krueger. This bill requires insurance companies to cover all contraceptives regardless of income! Now it's on to the PA State Senate and . Trump just made it even more dangerous for pregnant people needing emergency care The Trump administration just rescinded critical guidance that protected pregnant people in emergency rooms needing urgent abortion care. Adding to the confusion, this doesn't change the actual law but removes guidance that was essential for protecting patients in crisis and their doctors, especially in states with abortion bans. This move isn't about health or safety--it's about creating fear and barriers to care. Here's by the Center for Reproductive Rights | Union Yes! Retaliation No! When the workers at Allegheny Reproductive Health Center informed management of their intent to unionize, staff received no response other than to be denied voluntary union recognition. Since that time, two longtime staff members working to organize the have been fired in retaliation.This is unacceptable and hugely disappointing behavior from a leadership team that espouses inclusion and safety within their clinic. Disability Justice Digest Transportation KPEF's Disability Justice Director Mallory Hudson and Board Member Alisa Grishman (pictured) joined our colleagues in the in Harrisburg this week to tell our lawmakers: No State Budget Without Transit Funding For All! In all 67 counties of the Commonwealth, public transportation is a lifeline for our elders, youth, disabled community members, and anyone without access to a personal vehicle. Our coalition has one demand: Restore transit service across the state to 2019 levels --and expand transit service in communities outside of Pittsburgh and Philadelphia by an additional 10%. Getting our transit service up to 2019 levels in 2025 is achievable and, with your help, our state representatives will act! | Take Action Use this from Transit for All PA! to contact your Representatives, post social media graphics, and more. Upcoming Events 📆 June 11th: 📍 7:00 -8:30pm ✨ Virtual ➡️ Our local transit service needs state funding, and riders are organizing to win it! NPR is a left-wing propaganda outlet that shouldn’t get funded by __**YOUR TAX DOLLARS.**__

They dismiss conservatives and promote false liberal narratives, paid for with your hard-earned money. It’s wrong, and it’s time to stop it.

clean_body: NPR is a left-wing propaganda outlet that shouldn’t get funded by YOUR TAX DOLLARS. | Fellow Conservative, NPR is a left-wing propaganda outlet that shouldn’t get funded by YOUR TAX DOLLARS. They dismiss conservatives and promote false liberal narratives, paid for with your hard-earned money. It’s wrong, and it’s time to stop it. President Trump has taken bold action to END taxpayer funding for NPR, calling it out for spreading "radical, woke propaganda disguised as news." Now, NPR is suing President Trump over it. They think they’re entitled to your money. It’s like John King said a few days ago, "NPR doesn’t have a Constitutional right to your tax dollars." Together, we can send a clear message to Washington: Americans are done footing the bill for the liberal media! -- Team King Alerts | General John King is a soldier, lawman, and the first Hispanic elected statewide in Georgia history. He’s running for the Senate now because Georgians need a leader who will fight for them, fight for President Trump’s agenda, and put America First. | | | Paid For By John King For Senate Inc. name: Bonta HQ
subject: In case you missed it
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
# [](https://act.robbonta.com/go/3?refcode=em20250606&t=1&akid=1818%2E1088799%2EgKDszN)
In case you missed it, **Rob recently spoke with Rev. Al Sharpton on MSNBC** about President Trump and Republicans’ many unlawful threats to Californians and how he’s fighting back.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250606?refcode=em20250606-1818&amount=5&refcode2=1818_1088799_gKDszN&akid=1818%2E1088799%2EgKDszN)
The Trump administration announced plans to **aggressively revoke Chinese student visas, which could impact 50,000 students in California alone.** This is a discriminatory attack on students’ due process — Rob’s office is keeping a close watch on this and will get involved if the administration violates the Constitution.
Then, **48 California counties and dozens of cities were listed as “sanctuary cities” by the administration and put on notice** to start collaborating with ICE to enforce Trump’s mass deportation agenda. We’re focused on keeping communities safe, not carrying out federal immigration enforcement.
**Trump and Senate Republicans are also trying to revoke California’s clean vehicle waivers** , a misguided effort that is pro-pollution, anti-environment, anti-health, anti-innovation, and anti-California. It’s not just wrong: _it’s unlawful and has never been done before._ We’re the biggest state in the country and leaders in both the nation and the world on climate action and clean vehicle innovation — we won’t be bullied into moving backward, so we will sue to block this.
Under Rob’s leadership, California has brought 23 lawsuits against the Trump administration in 19 weeks. In a majority of those cases, we’re winning and blocking the president’s unlawful, unconstitutional actions from harming Californians.
**[When our rights are violated and the law is broken, we must fight back — but Rob can’t do it alone. Support the Attorney General’s fight by making a grassroots donation of $5 or more to our campaign today!](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250606?refcode=em20250606-1818&amount=5&refcode2=1818_1088799_gKDszN&akid=1818%2E1088799%2EgKDszN)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250606?refcode=em20250606-1818&amount=5&refcode2=1818_1088799_gKDszN&akid=1818%2E1088799%2EgKDszN)**
— Bonta HQ
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-wrapper?refcode=em20250606&t=3&refcode2=1818_1088799_gKDszN&akid=1818%2E1088799%2EgKDszN)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.robbonta.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=1818%2E1088799%2EgKDszN)

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.robbonta.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=1818%2E1088799%2EgKDszN)

To give by check:
Rob Bonta for Attorney General 2026
c/o Deane & Company
1700 Tribute Road, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95815
Email us: [info@robbonta.com](mailto:info@robbonta.com)

name: Jahana Hayes
subject: What I asked Secretary of Education Linda McMahon
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109114793/550048727/-1295403277?refcode=AU_JH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMWU4MjMzYi0wNDQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2zdgevatOKcEEFuNNe2LdmR3pg57-YKfLapXqhQwSqM=&emci=b78d1835-0443-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=11e8233b-0443-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15529135) | Team — This week, I questioned Secretary of Education Linda McMahon about cuts to the Department of Education. Her responses — when she gave them — were more than just disappointing. They were alarming. For many of my questions, she would not answer directly. And when she did answer, she was reading off cards handed to her by an advisor sitting next to her. _As a former teacher, if my students were cheating on their tests like that, they would get an F._ Furthermore, when I asked her whether teaching African-American history was considered a DEI program, she gave a hypocritical answer. We need to make sure students understand the deep history of our country — including African-American history. But if Secretary McMahon had her way, there would be no African-American history taught in schools. **Republican leaders are cutting funding for schools, changing the curriculum to suit their best interests, and hurting our kids in the process. It is despicable, and I am fighting back.** **[Please, chip in to help me stand up for students and teachers. I cannot do this work alone, I need you to have my back.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109114794/550048728/-1534047261?refcode=AU_JH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMWU4MjMzYi0wNDQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2zdgevatOKcEEFuNNe2LdmR3pg57-YKfLapXqhQwSqM=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [$10 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109114795/550048729/-1534047261?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_JH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250606_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMWU4MjMzYi0wNDQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2zdgevatOKcEEFuNNe2LdmR3pg57-YKfLapXqhQwSqM=)

clean_body: | | Team — This week, I questioned Secretary of Education Linda McMahon about cuts to the Department of Education. Her responses — when she gave them — were more than just disappointing. They were alarming. For many of my questions, she would not answer directly. And when she did answer, she was reading off cards handed to her by an advisor sitting next to her. As a former teacher, if my students were cheating on their tests like that, they would get an F. Furthermore, when I asked her whether teaching African-American history was considered a DEI program, she gave a hypocritical answer. We need to make sure students understand the deep history of our country — including African-American history. But if Secretary McMahon had her way, there would be no African-American history taught in schools. Republican leaders are cutting funding for schools, changing the curriculum to suit their best interests, and hurting our kids in the process. It is despicable, and I am fighting back. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you for your support, Jahana Hayes | | About Jahana** Jahana Hayes proudly represents Connecticut's 5th Congressional district. name: John Fetterman
subject: fighting for my LGBTQIA+ friends 🌈
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
Hey folks, it’s John, wishing you a Happy Pride Month! 🖤🤎❤️🧡💛💚💙💜

Every June, we honor the LGBTQIA+ community – not just with flags and parades, but by remembering that Pride started as an *uprising*. A fight against injustice. ✊

Pride is a reminder that freedom isn’t handed out. We gotta fight for it. And yinz know I’ve been in this fight for a loooong time.

Back in 2013, when same-sex marriage was still illegal in Pennsylvania, I didn’t wait around for permission – I put on my nice shirt and officiated a gay wedding in Allegheny County, because it was the right thing to do. 🏳️🌈

I caught some heat for it. But I didn’t care. Because to me, it’s simple: It doesn’t matter who you love or how you identify, we all deserve the same damn rights and freedoms as Americans. 💯

But let’s not pretend that everything’s fine. The Trump administration and their extremist cronies are going after LGBTQIA+ rights again – and it’s straight-up un-American.

I’m not just gonna sit back while they come for my friends. In the Senate, I’ve backed the Equality Act, I’ve pushed to protect trans kids, and I vote to defend LGBTQIA+ rights every single chance I get – because this fight is about *real* people. It's not about scoring cheap political points like some far-right Republicans are trying to do.

So yeah, Pride Month is a celebration. But it’s also a call to action. If you're queer and live in PA – or anywhere in this country – I want you to know: I've got your back. Always. 🌈

If you're with me in this fight to protect our LGBTQIA+ friends, and you want to do something about it, you can use this link or the button below to chip in $5 and stand up to this hateful MAGA bullsh*t:

https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51314?t=1001&akid=10129%2E434096%2EDIdfh9

Happy Pride to everyone. 🏳️🌈🏳️⚧️

Love yinz,

John ❤️

--------

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here: https://act.johnfetterman.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1002&akid=10129%2E434096%2EDIdfh9

name: Letitia James
subject: “A total crook,” “a horrible, horrible human being,” and “a sick person” – Donald Trump
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
I refuse to bow down to bullies. |
---
_Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109115458/550050554/-1590518309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjA4NGE4Ni0wNDQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bYAm5KdbedJuM9G2PFmHOiXP1PGC3utYvV6JEzaCnoQ=&emci=62c08821-5542-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=8f084a86-0443-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7878003)

Friend, recently, Trump attacked me live on TV at the Oval Office — this time calling me “a total crook,” “a horrible, horrible human being,” and “a sick person.” He even suggested the Department of Justice will come after me. = Let me be clear: This is not just another outburst. It’s part of a coordinated smear campaign – from Trump to Stephen Miller to Alex Jones to the full weight of the MAGA machine – all working to discredit me for doing my job and holding them accountable. name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: Lies, lies, and more lies.
body:
It's all the NRCC has against us.

| |
---
Friend, I've had Republicans say a lot about me over the years, and over the last couple of election cycles, it's only gotten worse. But last week, they stooped to new lows, putting dark money behind an ad that claimed I voted to cut Medicare and Social Security. **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109115674/550051200/-1968428927?refcode=250606_mk_lies_img&ms=250606_mk_lies&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZWE5YzFlZS0wNDQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gjoYc2DP6UZqanyRjoYPf8LzTTLSAzlP_zh58c_EcYE=)** The audacity to do this shortly after House Republicans forced through their budget for billionaires that cut healthcare and food for working families and seniors, stripping coverage from roughly 40,00 people across Northwest Ohio. **Let me be clear, team: I will always protect Social Security and Medicare. ** And while these ads are frustrating, they don't scare me. They embolden our team and the work we do. They show that our opponents have nothing to come after us with other than lies and deceit. **[That doesn't work in Northwest Ohio, and that's why we keep winning — and 2026 will be no different. Team,

Our campaign kicked into high gear in May, and Nellie was hard at work, with North Jersey in focus. Here's what we got up to:

About Nellie Pou
Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. As a mother and grandmother, Nellie knows what New Jersey families are facing and will continue to bring down costs while creating good-paying jobs. Nellie has dedicated her entire life to the community that raised her, and in Congress she will do the same.

name: Sharice Davids
subject: Fed up
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
I’ve seen far too many of my colleagues allow wealthy special interests to steer their priorities

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109116429/550053553/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiN2RmZmEwNi0wNzQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HaBN5sNgQhx8vcLUduFZbaD0YvXI5CiQXY_EGVhZLGU=) It’s no big secret that so many Americans have lost faith in their government, even before this new Trump administration. I hear from so many folks who are fed up with the system, who feel like Congress is only working for themselves, not the American people. And it’s not particularly difficult to see why. Instead of letting the values of their constituents guide their votes, I’ve seen far too many of my colleagues allow wealthy special interests to steer their priorities. The vast majority of Americans support common sense gun safety legislation. _**But special interest groups like the NRA have blocked reform at every turn.**_ And despite the fact that over 75% of Americans support Medicaid, _**Republicans in Congress are attempting to gut the program to appease their ultra-wealthy donors.**_ Transparency and accountability are essential to a well-functioning, trustworthy government. That’s why I’ve been one of the most vocal advocates to ban stock trading by members of Congress. I even co-sponsored a bill that would shine a light on dark money in politics, break special interests’ stranglehold on Congress, and close loopholes for lobbyists. **But if we want to restore trust in our government for good, we need to start by supporting real leaders in Congress, not more self-serving extremists.** [_**I don’t take marching orders from wealthy special interest groups. Never have, never will. If that kind of leadership speaks to you, will you rush a donation to help me flip the House blue next year and get Congress focused on the needs of the American people once again?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109116430/550053554/277100410?refcode=email_060625_fed-up&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiN2RmZmEwNi0wNzQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HaBN5sNgQhx8vcLUduFZbaD0YvXI5CiQXY_EGVhZLGU=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109116431/550053555/277100410?refcode=email_060625_fed-up&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=5&express_lane=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiN2RmZmEwNi0wNzQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HaBN5sNgQhx8vcLUduFZbaD0YvXI5CiQXY_EGVhZLGU=)

name: Heartland Patriots HQ
subject: Big, public, messy breakup
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
all while millions of Americans worried about whether politicians in DC would rip away their healthcare and food assistance
#
Peter — yesterday, as millions of Americans worried about whether or not politicians in DC would rip away their healthcare and food assistance, two of our country’s most out-of-touch billionaires engaged in a messy, public, online breakup.
It was mortifying. Those two have no idea what it’s like to live and survive as a real working person in this country — and frankly, they couldn’t care less. All they want is more power, more wealth, and more control — no matter who gets exploited in the process.
That’s why we’re fighting back, Peter.
**Heartland Patriots is dedicated to electing real working people to office — candidates who won’t back down when your rights, benefits, and livelihoods are on the line.**
But our mission isn’t funded by big checks from corporate executives and billionaires. Our grassroots movement relies on everyday people like you to make an impact.
**So we’re asking:[Will you chip in $10 or whatever you can today so we can elect real leaders who will fight like hell for working families? Peter –

Thank you so much for helping us reach our goal! We couldn’t have done it without your support.

I work hard in Congress every day for my constituents. I will not cower as extremism continues from the Trump administration and Congressional Republicans.

I will never stop fighting for our communities. Thank you again for your incredible support and for standing with me.

– Mike Thompson

name: Chris Pappas
subject: I’d be the first openly gay man in the U.S. Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek,
I’m Chris Pappas, Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate in New Hampshire. Tim Kaine said I could reach out to you today with a special message about Pride.
I’ve always admired Harvey Milk. He changed history and paved the way for folks like me when he became one of the first openly gay elected officials in America fifty years ago.
Since then there have been numerous other examples of LGBTQ+ leaders elected across the country, like Tammy Baldwin, Pete Buttigieg, and Sarah McBride, just to name a few.
These leaders have broken down barriers, stood up to discrimination, and fought for a vision of our country where every American can live with safety, dignity, and equality.
I’m proud to be a part of that legacy – and to carry the torch forward with this campaign.
If elected I’ll be the first openly gay man to ever serve in the U.S. Senate. And I will work as hard as I can when I’m in the Senate to ensure everyone – no matter who they are or who they love – has the freedom and opportunity to thrive, whether that means owning a home, starting a family, opening a small business, or anything else in between.
This Pride Month, I’m asking for your support to power this grassroots campaign. We’re on the verge of making history, but we can’t win this fight without a strong foundation.
Donald Trump and MAGA Republicans would like nothing more than to flip this pivotal Senate seat red next November – but if we fight back, we can stop them in their tracks.
Are you with me ? Donate $40 now to my campaign and Tim Kaine . Together we can keep this seat blue and make LGBTQ+ history at the same time. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-partner_ms_cp_em_kaine]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-partner_ms_cp_em_kaine?express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-partner_ms_cp_em_kaine?express_lane=true&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-partner_ms_cp_em_kaine?express_lane=true&amount=100]
DONATE OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-partner_ms_cp_em_kaine]
Happy Pride Month – and thank you for standing with me as we continue making history.
With gratitude,
Chris Pappas
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025-partner_ms_cp_em_kaine]
Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272
Williamsburg, VA 23185

name: Andrew Horne
subject: I’m Running for State House in LD4
body:
Dear Friends,

I’m excited to announce my candidacy for the Arizona State House in Legislative District 4.

I was born and raised right here in LD4. I attended Kiva, Mohave, Saguaro, and Arizona State University. This district shaped me, and now I’m here to serve the community that raised me.

I want to recognize the incredible work of Christine Marsh, Karen Gresham, and Kelli Butler. These leaders gave everything to fight for this district last cycle. I’m proud to build on the strong foundation they laid as we work to win this seat and move our community forward.

**Strengthening Public Education**
We must support our teachers, reduce turnover, and expand affordable education and job training through community colleges and trade schools.

**Growing Arizona’s Economy**
We will invest in infrastructure, support small businesses, and position our state as a national leader in microchip production, defense, and clean energy.

**Improving Quality of Life**
This means strengthening access to healthcare and ensuring essential services are there for working families, children, and seniors across Arizona.

It’s time to win this seat back and ensure that Legislative District 4 has the strong, local leadership it deserves.

You can help me get on the ballot by signing my petition here:
<https://go.azsos.gov/3rxc>

Let’s bring this seat home and build a stronger Arizona together.

Thank you,

Andrew Horne
480-256-9332[](http://voice.google.com/calls?a=nc,%2B14802569332 "Call +1 480-256-9332 via Google Voice")[](http://voice.google.com/calls?a=nc,%2B14802569332 "Call +1 480-256-9332 via Google Voice")
[www.HorneForArizona.com](http://www.HorneForArizona.com)
